file.content:
# Saga

## Saga -1: Repo scaffolding (COMPLETE)
Repo compartmentalization scaffolding.

## Saga 0: Foundation (COMPLETE)
Foundation and contracts.

## Saga 1: Dense tensor substrate v1 (COMPLETE)
Shape, DenseArray, reshape, transpose, indexing.

## Saga 2: Parser and evaluator foundation (COMPLETE)
Lexer, AST, parser with precedence, AST-walking evaluator.

## Saga 3: CLI and REPL v1 (COMPLETE)
Working REPL with all v1 syntax, built-in functions, tracing.

## Saga 4: Structured trace v1 (COMPLETE)
TraceEvent/Trace types, evaluator instrumentation, JSON export.

## Saga 5: Visual web viewer v1 (DEFERRED)
Deferred to post-MVP. MVP uses CLI + JSON trace export.

## MVP
Sagas 0 through 4 complete. MVP ships with REPL + trace export.

## Saga 6: ML foundations (COMPLETE)
Sigmoid, tanh_fn, pow, comparison operators, axis reductions, mean,
array constructors (zeros, ones, fill), and the first end-to-end
logistic regression demo. Delivered v0.2.

## Saga 7: SVG visualization v1 (COMPLETE)
`mlpl-viz` crate, `svg(data, type[, aux])` built-in, diagram types
(scatter, line, bar, heatmap, decision_boundary), `grid()` helper,
high-level analysis helpers (hist, scatter_labeled, loss_curve,
confusion_matrix, boundary_2d), browser REPL inline SVG rendering,
and download button. Delivered v0.3.

## Saga 10: Optimizers + training loop (COMPLETE)
Built proper training infrastructure on top of Saga 9 autograd.
New built-ins: `momentum_sgd(loss, params, lr, beta)` and
`adam(loss, params, lr, b1, b2, eps)` with per-parameter state
held in an `OptimizerState` map on the evaluation environment;
`cosine_schedule(step, total, lr_min, lr_max)` and
`linear_warmup(step, warmup, lr)` pure scalar schedules; and
two non-linear synthetic datasets `moons(seed, n, noise)` and
`circles(seed, n, noise)` returning `[N, 3]` `[x, y, label]`
matrices in the same layout as `blobs`. New `train N { body }`
language construct binds the iteration index to `step` inside
the body and captures each iteration's final value into a
`last_losses` 1-D array, replacing the manual
`repeat { grad; manual update; record loss }` recipe. Two new
demos -- `demos/moons_mlp.mlpl` and `demos/circles_mlp.mlpl` --
train a tanh MLP with `adam` inside `train { }` and render the
decision boundary with `boundary_2d`. New "Optimizers and
Schedules" tutorial lesson added to the web REPL. Delivered v0.6.

## Saga 9: Autograd v1 (COMPLETE)
Reverse-mode autograd as a language primitive. New `mlpl-autograd`
crate provides a tape-based `Tensor` with backward over add, sub,
mul, div, neg, exp, log, relu, tanh, sigmoid, softmax, sum, mean,
transpose, reshape, and matmul, all gradcheck-verified against
finite differences. Parser surface adds `param[shape]` and
`tensor[shape]` constructors, and a new `grad(expr, wrt)` built-in
lifts array expressions onto the tape and returns the gradient
with respect to a tracked parameter. The v0.4 `tiny_mlp` and
`softmax_classifier` demos were ported to use `param + grad`
instead of hand-written backprop, and a new "Automatic
Differentiation" tutorial lesson walks from a scalar minimization
to a one-layer linear regression. Delivered v0.5.

## Saga 8: ML demos (COMPLETE)
Synthetic data primitives (random, randn, argmax, blobs) and
higher-level ML built-ins (softmax, one_hot), plus six demos wired
into the browser REPL: k-means clustering, PCA via power iteration,
a linear softmax classifier, a tiny MLP on XOR-style data, and a
scaled dot-product attention pattern. Tutorial lessons added for
each. Delivered v0.4.

tag v0.7.0
Tagger: Mike Wright <mike@softwarewrighter.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 9 14:44:56 2026 -0700

Saga 11 Model DSL milestone: chain DSL, differentiable apply(), transformer demo, REPL introspection

commit 51ba09c050eca209f23739d02860bbd36b0a48c2
Author: Mike Wright <mike@softwarewrighter.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 9 14:44:49 2026 -0700

    chore: release v0.7.0 -- Saga 11 model DSL milestone
    
    Bumps the workspace version from 0.1.0 to 0.7.0 and updates the
    mlpl-repl and mlpl-web banner strings to match. This commit marks
    the v0.7.0 release point: the Saga 11 Model DSL is feature-complete
    on the user-facing surface (linear, chain, activations, residual,
    rms_norm, single-head attention, differentiable apply()), the
    moons_mlp / tiny_mlp / transformer_block demos all train end-to-end
    through the DSL, the Model Composition tutorial lesson is in the
    web REPL, and the new :vars / :models / :fns / :wsid / :describe
    introspection commands ship in both REPLs.
    
    Quality gates at this commit:
    - cargo build --release: clean
    - cargo clippy --release --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings: clean
    - cargo test --release: 82 suites green, 0 failures
    - cargo fmt --all -- --check: clean
    
    Pages rebuilt with the new v0.7.0 banner.
    
    Multi-head attention tape lowering, named-axes (Saga 11.5), and the
    remaining "agent ergonomics" gaps stay as planned future work.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.content:
1	# MLPL Multi-Saga Plan
2	
3	This plan sequences the remaining work from `docs/research2.txt`
4	(the small-LLM / training-platform vision) against the current
5	state of the repo. Completed sagas are listed in `docs/saga.md`;
6	through v0.4 we have the language core, REPL, SVG viz, ML
7	built-ins, and six end-to-end demos, but every demo still carries
8	its own hand-written gradients. Everything downstream of autograd
9	depends on removing that limitation.
10	
11	## Guiding principles
12	
13	- Each saga delivers a visible user-facing win (a demo or a
14	  workflow), not just plumbing.
15	- Preserve the "thinking space" loop: train -> visualize ->
16	  adjust. Visualization is core syntax, not an afterthought.
17	- Backends (MLX, CUDA) come only after the CPU path is correct,
18	  tested, and expressive enough to be worth accelerating.
19	- Keep the cellular crate layout; new capabilities land as new
20	  crates, not as bulges in existing ones.
21	- TDD + quality gates per CLAUDE.md on every step.
22	
23	## Completed sagas (context)
24	
25	- **Saga -1** Repo scaffolding
26	- **Saga 0** Foundation and contracts
27	- **Saga 1** Dense tensor substrate v1 (Shape, DenseArray, reshape, transpose, indexing)
28	- **Saga 2** Parser and evaluator foundation
29	- **Saga 3** CLI and REPL v1
30	- **Saga 4** Structured trace v1 (JSON export)
31	- **Saga 5** Visual web viewer v1 -- DEFERRED
32	- **Saga 6** ML foundations (v0.2): sigmoid, tanh, pow, comparisons,
33	  axis reductions, mean, zeros/ones/fill, logistic regression demo
34	- **Saga 7** SVG visualization v1 (v0.3): `mlpl-viz`, `svg()`,
35	  scatter/line/bar/heatmap/decision_boundary, hist, scatter_labeled,
36	  loss_curve, confusion_matrix, boundary_2d, browser REPL inline SVG
37	- **Saga 8** ML demos (v0.4): random/randn/argmax/blobs/softmax/one_hot;
38	  k-means, PCA, softmax classifier, tiny MLP, attention pattern demos;
39	  tutorial lessons for each
40	- **Saga 9** Autograd v1 (v0.5): `mlpl-autograd` reverse-mode tape;
41	  `param[...]` / `tensor[...]` constructors; `grad(expr, wrt)` built-in;
42	  tiny MLP and softmax classifier demos ported off hand-written backprop;
43	  "Automatic differentiation" tutorial lesson
44	- **Saga 10** Optimizers + training loop (v0.6): `momentum_sgd` and
45	  `adam` built-ins with per-param state on the environment;
46	  `cosine_schedule` / `linear_warmup` scalar helpers; `moons` and
47	  `circles` synthetic datasets; new `train N { body }` construct
48	  with implicit `step` binding and `last_losses` capture;
49	  `moons_mlp` and `circles_mlp` demos; "Optimizers and Schedules"
50	  tutorial lesson
51	
52	## Future saga sequence
53	
54	### Saga 11 -- Model DSL (NEXT)
55	`chain[...]`, `residual[...]`, `norm[...]`, `attention[...]`
56	combinators. Parameter trees with named addressing. Rebuild the
57	tiny MLP and attention demos using the DSL; add a 2-layer
58	transformer block demo (still CPU, still tiny).
59	
60	### Saga 11.5 -- Named axes and shape introspection
61	Labeled shape metadata on `Value::Array`, annotation syntax on
62	assignment (`x : [batch, time, dim] = ...`), label propagation
63	through matmul/elementwise/reductions, structured `ShapeMismatch`
64	errors, and agent-facing introspection commands (`:describe`,
65	`:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`). Inserted between Saga 11 and Saga 12
66	because every later saga (Tiny LM, LoRA, attention variants,
67	embedding viz) benefits from labeled axes. Surface-only milestone:
68	no new kernels, no new backends, CPU only. See
69	`docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.
70	
71	### Saga 12 -- Tokenizers, datasets, and experiment tracking
72	Streaming/lazy dataset ops (`load`, `tokenize`, `shuffle`,
73	`batch`), a byte-level BPE tokenizer, and `experiment "name"`
74	objects with seed control, config snapshots, and logged metrics.
75	Reproducibility story lands here.
76	
77	### Saga 13 -- Tiny LM end-to-end
78	Train a character-level or tiny-BPE language model (~1-5M params)
79	on a small corpus entirely in MLPL on CPU. Visualize loss,
80	sample generations, and attention maps. This is the first saga
81	that proves the platform thesis end-to-end.
82	
83	### Saga 14 -- MLX backend
84	Backend abstraction layer (`backend <- mlx | cpu`), lazy
85	execution graph, kernel fusion for the hot ops. Re-run the tiny
86	LM on Apple Silicon and show a concrete speedup. CUDA deferred.
87	
88	### Saga 15 -- LoRA / QLoRA and quantization
89	`lora[rank=8] model`, layer-scoped adapters, shared subspaces,
90	4-bit quantization for the frozen base. Fine-tune the Saga 13
91	model on a small instruction set.
92	
93	### Saga 16 -- Embedding visualization and RAG
94	`embed`, `plot pca/tsne/umap`, 3D scatter via SVG projection,
95	nearest-neighbor links. RAG pipeline demo over a small corpus.
96	Builds directly on the existing viz stack.
97	
98	### Saga 17 -- CUDA backend and distributed execution
99	CUDA kernels for the fused ops from Saga 14; `run model on
100	nodes[...]` primitives; device placement syntax. Homelab LAN
101	training demo.
102	
103	### Saga 18 -- Distillation, ICL/ICRL, engram memory
104	Distillation pipelines, trajectory-based updates, engram-style
105	memory attachments, and multi-model orchestration
106	(`orchestrate[planner, executor, verifier]`). The research
107	frontier items from section 6 of `research2.txt`.
108	
109	### Saga 19 -- LLM-as-tool integration
110	REST client built-ins, teacher-model distillation workflows,
111	codegen helpers. Intentionally last: secondary to the
112	"build your own model" story.
113	
114	## Dependency graph (abbreviated)
115	
116	```
117	9 autograd
118	  |-- 10 optimizers -- 11 model DSL -- 11.5 named axes -- 12 data+tracking -- 13 tiny LM
119	  |                                                                                |
120	  |                                                                                +-- 14 MLX
121	  |                                                                                +-- 15 LoRA
122	  |                                                                                +-- 16 viz/RAG
123	  |                                                                                +-- 17 CUDA/dist
124	  |                                                                                +-- 18 distill/ICRL
125	  |                                                                                +-- 19 LLM REST
126	```
127	
128	Sagas 14-19 can reorder based on hardware access and interest;
129	9-13 (plus the inserted 11.5) must run in order.
130	
131	## Start next: finish Saga 11, then Saga 11.5 -- Named axes
132	
133	Saga 11 (Model DSL) is mostly shipped: model values, atomic and
134	composed layers, params walker, residual + rms_norm + attention,
135	differentiable `apply()` through the tape (Linear/Chain/Activation,
136	then also Residual/RmsNorm/single-head Attention as of step 007),

# MLPL Multi-Saga Plan

This plan sequences the remaining work from `docs/research2.txt`
(the small-LLM / training-platform vision) against the current
state of the repo. Completed sagas are listed in `docs/saga.md`;
through v0.4 we have the language core, REPL, SVG viz, ML
built-ins, and six end-to-end demos, but every demo still carries
its own hand-written gradients. Everything downstream of autograd
depends on removing that limitation.

## Guiding principles

- Each saga delivers a visible user-facing win (a demo or a
  workflow), not just plumbing.
- Preserve the "thinking space" loop: train -> visualize ->
  adjust. Visualization is core syntax, not an afterthought.
- Backends (MLX, CUDA) come only after the CPU path is correct,
  tested, and expressive enough to be worth accelerating.
- Keep the cellular crate layout; new capabilities land as new
  crates, not as bulges in existing ones.
- TDD + quality gates per CLAUDE.md on every step.

## Completed sagas (context)

- **Saga -1** Repo scaffolding
- **Saga 0** Foundation and contracts
- **Saga 1** Dense tensor substrate v1 (Shape, DenseArray, reshape, transpose, indexing)
- **Saga 2** Parser and evaluator foundation
- **Saga 3** CLI and REPL v1
- **Saga 4** Structured trace v1 (JSON export)
- **Saga 5** Visual web viewer v1 -- DEFERRED
- **Saga 6** ML foundations (v0.2): sigmoid, tanh, pow, comparisons,
  axis reductions, mean, zeros/ones/fill, logistic regression demo
- **Saga 7** SVG visualization v1 (v0.3): `mlpl-viz`, `svg()`,
  scatter/line/bar/heatmap/decision_boundary, hist, scatter_labeled,
  loss_curve, confusion_matrix, boundary_2d, browser REPL inline SVG
- **Saga 8** ML demos (v0.4): random/randn/argmax/blobs/softmax/one_hot;
  k-means, PCA, softmax classifier, tiny MLP, attention pattern demos;
  tutorial lessons for each
- **Saga 9** Autograd v1 (v0.5): `mlpl-autograd` reverse-mode tape;
  `param[...]` / `tensor[...]` constructors; `grad(expr, wrt)` built-in;
  tiny MLP and softmax classifier demos ported off hand-written backprop;
  "Automatic differentiation" tutorial lesson
- **Saga 10** Optimizers + training loop (v0.6): `momentum_sgd` and
  `adam` built-ins with per-param state on the environment;
  `cosine_schedule` / `linear_warmup` scalar helpers; `moons` and
  `circles` synthetic datasets; new `train N { body }` construct
  with implicit `step` binding and `last_losses` capture;
  `moons_mlp` and `circles_mlp` demos; "Optimizers and Schedules"
  tutorial lesson

## Future saga sequence

### Saga 11 -- Model DSL (NEXT)
`chain[...]`, `residual[...]`, `norm[...]`, `attention[...]`
combinators. Parameter trees with named addressing. Rebuild the
tiny MLP and attention demos using the DSL; add a 2-layer
transformer block demo (still CPU, still tiny).

### Saga 11.5 -- Named axes and shape introspection
Labeled shape metadata on `Value::Array`, annotation syntax on
assignment (`x : [batch, time, dim] = ...`), label propagation
through matmul/elementwise/reductions, structured `ShapeMismatch`
errors, and agent-facing introspection commands (`:describe`,
`:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`). Inserted between Saga 11 and Saga 12
because every later saga (Tiny LM, LoRA, attention variants,
embedding viz) benefits from labeled axes. Surface-only milestone:
no new kernels, no new backends, CPU only. See
`docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

### Saga 12 -- Tokenizers, datasets, and experiment tracking
Streaming/lazy dataset ops (`load`, `tokenize`, `shuffle`,
`batch`), a byte-level BPE tokenizer, and `experiment "name"`
objects with seed control, config snapshots, and logged metrics.
Reproducibility story lands here.

### Saga 13 -- Tiny LM end-to-end
Train a character-level or tiny-BPE language model (~1-5M params)
on a small corpus entirely in MLPL on CPU. Visualize loss,
sample generations, and attention maps. This is the first saga
that proves the platform thesis end-to-end.

### Saga 14 -- MLX backend
Backend abstraction layer (`backend <- mlx | cpu`), lazy
execution graph, kernel fusion for the hot ops. Re-run the tiny
LM on Apple Silicon and show a concrete speedup. CUDA deferred.

### Saga 15 -- LoRA / QLoRA and quantization
`lora[rank=8] model`, layer-scoped adapters, shared subspaces,
4-bit quantization for the frozen base. Fine-tune the Saga 13
model on a small instruction set.

### Saga 16 -- Embedding visualization and RAG
`embed`, `plot pca/tsne/umap`, 3D scatter via SVG projection,
nearest-neighbor links. RAG pipeline demo over a small corpus.
Builds directly on the existing viz stack.

### Saga 17 -- CUDA backend and distributed execution
CUDA kernels for the fused ops from Saga 14; `run model on
nodes[...]` primitives; device placement syntax. Homelab LAN
training demo.

### Saga 18 -- Distillation, ICL/ICRL, engram memory
Distillation pipelines, trajectory-based updates, engram-style
memory attachments, and multi-model orchestration
(`orchestrate[planner, executor, verifier]`). The research
frontier items from section 6 of `research2.txt`.

### Saga 19 -- LLM-as-tool integration
REST client built-ins, teacher-model distillation workflows,
codegen helpers. Intentionally last: secondary to the
"build your own model" story.

## Dependency graph (abbreviated)

```
9 autograd
  |-- 10 optimizers -- 11 model DSL -- 11.5 named axes -- 12 data+tracking -- 13 tiny LM
  |                                                                                |
  |                                                                                +-- 14 MLX
  |                                                                                +-- 15 LoRA
  |                                                                                +-- 16 viz/RAG
  |                                                                                +-- 17 CUDA/dist
  |                                                                                +-- 18 distill/ICRL
  |                                                                                +-- 19 LLM REST
```

Sagas 14-19 can reorder based on hardware access and interest;
9-13 (plus the inserted 11.5) must run in order.

## Start next: finish Saga 11, then Saga 11.5 -- Named axes

Saga 11 (Model DSL) is mostly shipped: model values, atomic and
composed layers, params walker, residual + rms_norm + attention,
differentiable `apply()` through the tape (Linear/Chain/Activation,
then also Residual/RmsNorm/single-head Attention as of step 007),
and ported MLP + transformer-block demos. Remaining Saga 11 work
is the tutorial lesson (done ahead of schedule on 2026-04-09) and
the v0.7.0 release.

After Saga 11 ships as v0.7.0, Saga 11.5 adds labeled shapes and
introspection -- the surface-only milestone surfaced by
`docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` as the single biggest agent-usability
gap. See `docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

1	# Milestone: Optimizers + training loop (v0.6)
2	
3	Saga 10. Built proper training infrastructure on top of the
4	v0.5 autograd primitive: real optimizers with persistent state,
5	learning-rate schedules, a structured training loop construct,
6	and two non-linear synthetic datasets.
7	
8	## What shipped
9	
10	### Optimizers (mlpl-eval)
11	- `momentum_sgd(loss_expr, params, lr, beta)` -- heavy-ball
12	  momentum SGD with per-parameter velocity buffers stored on
13	  the evaluation environment.
14	- `adam(loss_expr, params, lr, b1, b2, eps)` -- standard Adam
15	  with bias correction and per-parameter first/second moment
16	  buffers, plus a per-optimizer step counter.
17	- Both surfaces accept either a single param identifier or an
18	  array literal of identifiers (`[W1, b1, W2, b2]`).
19	- State lives in a new `OptimizerState` map on `Environment`,
20	  keyed by `(optimizer_name, param_name, slot_name)`. Calling
21	  the same optimizer multiple times persists state across
22	  calls without any explicit threading from the program.
23	
24	### Schedules (mlpl-runtime)
25	- `cosine_schedule(step, total, lr_min, lr_max)`: cosine decay
26	  between `lr_max` (at step 0) and `lr_min` (at step total),
27	  clamped outside the range.
28	- `linear_warmup(step, warmup, lr)`: linear ramp from 0 to
29	  `lr` over the first `warmup` steps, clamped at `lr` after.
30	- Both are pure scalar functions with no state.
31	
32	### Datasets (mlpl-runtime)
33	- `moons(seed, n, noise)` -- two interleaving half-circles,
34	  the classic `make_moons` shape.
35	- `circles(seed, n, noise)` -- inner ring (radius 0.5, label 0)
36	  inside an outer ring (radius 1.0, label 1).
37	- Both return `[N, 3]` matrices in the same `[x, y, label]`
38	  layout as `blobs()`, deterministic given seed.
39	
40	### Training-loop sugar (mlpl-parser, mlpl-eval)
41	- New `train N { body }` statement form, parallel to `repeat`.
42	- Inside the body the iteration index is bound to `step`.
43	- The value of the body's final expression on each iteration
44	  is captured into a 1-D array stored in the environment as
45	  `last_losses` after the loop. Non-scalar final values are
46	  mean-reduced for the loss curve.
47	- Replaces the manual `repeat { grad; manual update; record
48	  loss into mask }` recipe used in v0.5 demos.
49	
50	### Demos
51	- `demos/moons_mlp.mlpl` -- 2 -> 8 -> 2 tanh MLP trained with
52	  Adam inside `train { }` on the moons dataset. Renders the
53	  decision boundary with `boundary_2d` over a 30x30 grid.
54	- `demos/circles_mlp.mlpl` -- companion demo on the circles
55	  dataset with a wider 2 -> 16 -> 2 hidden layer.
56	
57	### Tutorial
58	- New "Optimizers and Schedules" lesson in
59	  `apps/mlpl-web/src/tutorial.rs` walking from a single-param
60	  Adam quadratic through `train { }` and `last_losses` to
61	  cosine and warmup schedule samples.
62	
63	## What did not change
64	
65	- No backend changes -- still tree-walked CPU evaluation.
66	- No model DSL or parameter trees -- params are still flat
67	  identifiers in the environment. That work belongs to Saga 11.
68	- `grad()`'s tape walker still doesn't support `reduce_add` /
69	  `one_hot` / cross-entropy directly, so the loss inside
70	  `adam()` calls in the demos uses MSE on `softmax(...) - Y`
71	  (same constraint as the v0.5 `tiny_mlp` demo).
72	
73	## Notes for the next saga
74	
75	Saga 11 (Model DSL) should give us a way to express
76	`chain[linear(2, 16), tanh, linear(16, 2), softmax]` as a
77	single value with named parameters, which would let demos drop
78	their long fused matmul expressions and let optimizers walk a
79	parameter tree instead of an explicit identifier list. The
80	`OptimizerState` keyed-tuple storage should generalize to
81	hierarchical parameter names without changes.

file.content:
# Milestone: Optimizers + training loop (v0.6)

Saga 10. Built proper training infrastructure on top of the
v0.5 autograd primitive: real optimizers with persistent state,
learning-rate schedules, a structured training loop construct,
and two non-linear synthetic datasets.

## What shipped

### Optimizers (mlpl-eval)
- `momentum_sgd(loss_expr, params, lr, beta)` -- heavy-ball
  momentum SGD with per-parameter velocity buffers stored on
  the evaluation environment.
- `adam(loss_expr, params, lr, b1, b2, eps)` -- standard Adam
  with bias correction and per-parameter first/second moment
  buffers, plus a per-optimizer step counter.
- Both surfaces accept either a single param identifier or an
  array literal of identifiers (`[W1, b1, W2, b2]`).
- State lives in a new `OptimizerState` map on `Environment`,
  keyed by `(optimizer_name, param_name, slot_name)`. Calling
  the same optimizer multiple times persists state across
  calls without any explicit threading from the program.

### Schedules (mlpl-runtime)
- `cosine_schedule(step, total, lr_min, lr_max)`: cosine decay
  between `lr_max` (at step 0) and `lr_min` (at step total),
  clamped outside the range.
- `linear_warmup(step, warmup, lr)`: linear ramp from 0 to
  `lr` over the first `warmup` steps, clamped at `lr` after.
- Both are pure scalar functions with no state.

### Datasets (mlpl-runtime)
- `moons(seed, n, noise)` -- two interleaving half-circles,
  the classic `make_moons` shape.
- `circles(seed, n, noise)` -- inner ring (radius 0.5, label 0)
  inside an outer ring (radius 1.0, label 1).
- Both return `[N, 3]` matrices in the same `[x, y, label]`
  layout as `blobs()`, deterministic given seed.

### Training-loop sugar (mlpl-parser, mlpl-eval)
- New `train N { body }` statement form, parallel to `repeat`.
- Inside the body the iteration index is bound to `step`.
- The value of the body's final expression on each iteration
  is captured into a 1-D array stored in the environment as
  `last_losses` after the loop. Non-scalar final values are
  mean-reduced for the loss curve.
- Replaces the manual `repeat { grad; manual update; record
  loss into mask }` recipe used in v0.5 demos.

### Demos
- `demos/moons_mlp.mlpl` -- 2 -> 8 -> 2 tanh MLP trained with
  Adam inside `train { }` on the moons dataset. Renders the
  decision boundary with `boundary_2d` over a 30x30 grid.
- `demos/circles_mlp.mlpl` -- companion demo on the circles
  dataset with a wider 2 -> 16 -> 2 hidden layer.

### Tutorial
- New "Optimizers and Schedules" lesson in
  `apps/mlpl-web/src/tutorial.rs` walking from a single-param
  Adam quadratic through `train { }` and `last_losses` to
  cosine and warmup schedule samples.

## What did not change

- No backend changes -- still tree-walked CPU evaluation.
- No model DSL or parameter trees -- params are still flat
  identifiers in the environment. That work belongs to Saga 11.
- `grad()`'s tape walker still doesn't support `reduce_add` /
  `one_hot` / cross-entropy directly, so the loss inside
  `adam()` calls in the demos uses MSE on `softmax(...) - Y`
  (same constraint as the v0.5 `tiny_mlp` demo).

## Notes for the next saga

Saga 11 (Model DSL) should give us a way to express
`chain[linear(2, 16), tanh, linear(16, 2), softmax]` as a
single value with named parameters, which would let demos drop
their long fused matmul expressions and let optimizers walk a
parameter tree instead of an explicit identifier list. The
`OptimizerState` keyed-tuple storage should generalize to
hierarchical parameter names without changes.

76	a linear softmax classifier, a tiny MLP on XOR-style data, and a
77	scaled dot-product attention pattern. Tutorial lessons added for
78	each. Delivered v0.4.

Built proper training infrastructure on top of Saga 9 autograd.
New built-ins: `momentum_sgd(loss, params, lr, beta)` and
`adam(loss, params, lr, b1, b2, eps)` with per-parameter state
held in an `OptimizerState` map on the evaluation environment;
`cosine_schedule(step, total, lr_min, lr_max)` and
`linear_warmup(step, warmup, lr)` pure scalar schedules; and
two non-linear synthetic datasets `moons(seed, n, noise)` and
`circles(seed, n, noise)` returning `[N, 3]` `[x, y, label]`
matrices in the same layout as `blobs`. New `train N { body }`
language construct binds the iteration index to `step` inside
the body and captures each iteration's final value into a
`last_losses` 1-D array, replacing the manual
`repeat { grad; manual update; record loss }` recipe. Two new
demos -- `demos/moons_mlp.mlpl` and `demos/circles_mlp.mlpl` --
train a tanh MLP with `adam` inside `train { }` and render the
decision boundary with `boundary_2d`. New "Optimizers and
Schedules" tutorial lesson added to the web REPL. Delivered v0.6.

## Saga 9: Autograd v1 (COMPLETE)
Reverse-mode autograd as a language primitive. New `mlpl-autograd`
crate provides a tape-based `Tensor` with backward over add, sub,
mul, div, neg, exp, log, relu, tanh, sigmoid, softmax, sum, mean,
transpose, reshape, and matmul, all gradcheck-verified against
finite differences. Parser surface adds `param[shape]` and
`tensor[shape]` constructors, and a new `grad(expr, wrt)` built-in
lifts array expressions onto the tape and returns the gradient
with respect to a tracked parameter. The v0.4 `tiny_mlp` and
`softmax_classifier` demos were ported to use `param + grad`
instead of hand-written backprop, and a new "Automatic
Differentiation" tutorial lesson walks from a scalar minimization
to a one-layer linear regression. Delivered v0.5.

## Saga 8: ML demos (COMPLETE)
Synthetic data primitives (random, randn, argmax, blobs) and
higher-level ML built-ins (softmax, one_hot), plus six demos wired
into the browser REPL: k-means clustering, PCA via power iteration,
a linear softmax classifier, a tiny MLP on XOR-style data, and a
scaled dot-product attention pattern. Tutorial lessons added for
each. Delivered v0.4.

input.content:
# Milestone: Model DSL (v0.7)

Saga 11. Built composition primitives on top of the v0.6 optimizer
and training-loop infrastructure so neural-net models can be
expressed as data instead of long fused matmul expressions. After
this milestone, optimizers walk a parameter tree instead of a flat
identifier list, and the ported MLP / transformer demos read like
model definitions rather than loss-expression novellas.

## What shipped

### Model value type (mlpl-eval)
- New `Value::Model` variant carrying a callable layer description
  plus an attached parameter tree. Lives alongside `Value::Array`
  and the other runtime values; prints as a short tag in the REPL
  and is walkable by `:models` introspection.
- `apply(model, X)` built-in runs the forward pass. `apply` is
  also the entry point `grad()` uses when a tape walk sees a
  model on the left of a matmul / residual / norm / attention
  expression, so optimizers can differentiate straight through
  `apply(mdl, X)` without any identifier-list plumbing.

### Atomic layers
- `linear(in, out, seed)` -- `W : [in, out]`, `b : [1, out]`,
  deterministic init from the seed.
- Parameter-free activation layers: `tanh_layer()`, `relu_layer()`,
  `softmax_layer()`. These exist as first-class layers so a chain
  can be pure data instead of a mix of layers and raw ops.

### Composition combinators
- `chain(layer1, layer2, ...)` -- sequential composition. Accepts
  any number of child layers; nesting is fine.
- `residual(block)` -- skip connection: `y = x + block(x)`.
- `params(model)` -- flat walker returning every `param`
  identifier the model owns, suitable as the second argument to
  `momentum_sgd` / `adam`. In practice optimizers now accept the
  model directly and call `params()` internally, so
  `adam(loss, mdl, lr, b1, b2, eps)` is the common form.

### Normalization and attention
- `rms_norm(dim)` -- per-row RMS normalization, parameter-free.
  Tape-lowered to `x * rsqrt(mean(x*x))` where `rsqrt` is encoded
  as `exp(-0.5 * log(y))` so the existing autograd tape handles
  the backward pass without a new primitive.
- `attention(d_model, heads, seed)` -- multi-head self-attention
  with Q/K/V/output projections. `heads=1` is tape-lowered for
  end-to-end training; `heads>1` evaluates forward but returns
  `Unsupported` on the backward pass until per-head slicing
  lands in the tensor op surface.

### Ported demos
- `demos/moons_mlp.mlpl` and `demos/tiny_mlp.mlpl` rewritten as
  one-line `chain(linear, tanh_layer, linear, ...)` expressions
  trained inside `train 100 { adam(..., mdl, ...); loss }`.
  Source line counts dropped meaningfully relative to their v0.6
  form without losing accuracy on moons / blobs respectively.
- `demos/transformer_block.mlpl` -- new tiny 2-layer transformer
  block that stacks `residual(chain(rms_norm, attention))` and
  `residual(chain(rms_norm, linear, relu_layer, linear))` twice,
  followed by a final linear projection. Trains end-to-end on a
  random-input / random-target toy task with Adam for 100 steps;
  measured loss 143.87 -> 1.02 with a strictly monotonic decrease.

### REPL introspection
- `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`, `:wsid`, `:describe` commands in
  both `mlpl-repl` and `mlpl-web`, so you can inspect the
  parameter tree of a model value from the REPL without parsing
  through `shape(params(mdl))` by hand.
- `:fns` split into user and built-in sections with `:help
  <topic>` dispatch for longer explanations.

### Tutorial
- New "Model Composition (the Model DSL)" lesson in
  `apps/mlpl-web/src/tutorial.rs` walking from a single
  `chain(linear, tanh_layer, linear)` MLP through `apply(mdl, X)`
  to Adam inside `train { }` on the moons dataset, with a
  decision-boundary render at the end.

## What did not change

- No backend changes -- still tree-walked CPU evaluation.
- No new training-loop sugar. Saga 10's `train N { body }` does
  the full job once you have a model value; the saga's goal was
  purely surface-level.
- No tokenizer / dataset work -- that is Saga 12.
- Multi-head attention is forward-only; the tape lowering for
  `heads > 1` needs per-head slicing primitives that are not
  yet in the tensor op surface.

## Notes for the next saga

Saga 11.5 (Named Axes and Shape Introspection) is the next
surface-only milestone: labeled shape metadata on `Value::Array`,
annotation syntax on assignment (`x : [batch, time, dim] = ...`),
label propagation through matmul/elementwise/reductions, and
structured `ShapeMismatch` errors. It is inserted between Saga 11
and Saga 12 because every later saga (Tiny LM, LoRA, attention
variants, embedding viz) benefits from labeled axes. See
`docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

After 11.5 ships as v0.7.5, Saga 12 brings the streaming dataset
ops, byte-level BPE tokenizer, and `experiment "name"` tracking
that the tiny-LM saga depends on.

toolUseResult.content:
# Milestone: Model DSL (v0.7)

Saga 11. Built composition primitives on top of the v0.6 optimizer
and training-loop infrastructure so neural-net models can be
expressed as data instead of long fused matmul expressions. After
this milestone, optimizers walk a parameter tree instead of a flat
identifier list, and the ported MLP / transformer demos read like
model definitions rather than loss-expression novellas.

## What shipped

### Model value type (mlpl-eval)
- New `Value::Model` variant carrying a callable layer description
  plus an attached parameter tree. Lives alongside `Value::Array`
  and the other runtime values; prints as a short tag in the REPL
  and is walkable by `:models` introspection.
- `apply(model, X)` built-in runs the forward pass. `apply` is
  also the entry point `grad()` uses when a tape walk sees a
  model on the left of a matmul / residual / norm / attention
  expression, so optimizers can differentiate straight through
  `apply(mdl, X)` without any identifier-list plumbing.

### Atomic layers
- `linear(in, out, seed)` -- `W : [in, out]`, `b : [1, out]`,
  deterministic init from the seed.
- Parameter-free activation layers: `tanh_layer()`, `relu_layer()`,
  `softmax_layer()`. These exist as first-class layers so a chain
  can be pure data instead of a mix of layers and raw ops.

### Composition combinators
- `chain(layer1, layer2, ...)` -- sequential composition. Accepts
  any number of child layers; nesting is fine.
- `residual(block)` -- skip connection: `y = x + block(x)`.
- `params(model)` -- flat walker returning every `param`
  identifier the model owns, suitable as the second argument to
  `momentum_sgd` / `adam`. In practice optimizers now accept the
  model directly and call `params()` internally, so
  `adam(loss, mdl, lr, b1, b2, eps)` is the common form.

### Normalization and attention
- `rms_norm(dim)` -- per-row RMS normalization, parameter-free.
  Tape-lowered to `x * rsqrt(mean(x*x))` where `rsqrt` is encoded
  as `exp(-0.5 * log(y))` so the existing autograd tape handles
  the backward pass without a new primitive.
- `attention(d_model, heads, seed)` -- multi-head self-attention
  with Q/K/V/output projections. `heads=1` is tape-lowered for
  end-to-end training; `heads>1` evaluates forward but returns
  `Unsupported` on the backward pass until per-head slicing
  lands in the tensor op surface.

### Ported demos
- `demos/moons_mlp.mlpl` and `demos/tiny_mlp.mlpl` rewritten as
  one-line `chain(linear, tanh_layer, linear, ...)` expressions
  trained inside `train 100 { adam(..., mdl, ...); loss }`.
  Source line counts dropped meaningfully relative to their v0.6
  form without losing accuracy on moons / blobs respectively.
- `demos/transformer_block.mlpl` -- new tiny 2-layer transformer
  block that stacks `residual(chain(rms_norm, attention))` and
  `residual(chain(rms_norm, linear, relu_layer, linear))` twice,
  followed by a final linear projection. Trains end-to-end on a
  random-input / random-target toy task with Adam for 100 steps;
  measured loss 143.87 -> 1.02 with a strictly monotonic decrease.

### REPL introspection
- `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`, `:wsid`, `:describe` commands in
  both `mlpl-repl` and `mlpl-web`, so you can inspect the
  parameter tree of a model value from the REPL without parsing
  through `shape(params(mdl))` by hand.
- `:fns` split into user and built-in sections with `:help
  <topic>` dispatch for longer explanations.

### Tutorial
- New "Model Composition (the Model DSL)" lesson in
  `apps/mlpl-web/src/tutorial.rs` walking from a single
  `chain(linear, tanh_layer, linear)` MLP through `apply(mdl, X)`
  to Adam inside `train { }` on the moons dataset, with a
  decision-boundary render at the end.

## What did not change

- No backend changes -- still tree-walked CPU evaluation.
- No new training-loop sugar. Saga 10's `train N { body }` does
  the full job once you have a model value; the saga's goal was
  purely surface-level.
- No tokenizer / dataset work -- that is Saga 12.
- Multi-head attention is forward-only; the tape lowering for
  `heads > 1` needs per-head slicing primitives that are not
  yet in the tensor op surface.

## Notes for the next saga

Saga 11.5 (Named Axes and Shape Introspection) is the next
surface-only milestone: labeled shape metadata on `Value::Array`,
annotation syntax on assignment (`x : [batch, time, dim] = ...`),
label propagation through matmul/elementwise/reductions, and
structured `ShapeMismatch` errors. It is inserted between Saga 11
and Saga 12 because every later saga (Tiny LM, LoRA, attention
variants, embedding viz) benefits from labeled axes. See
`docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

After 11.5 ships as v0.7.5, Saga 12 brings the streaming dataset
ops, byte-level BPE tokenizer, and `experiment "name"` tracking
that the tiny-LM saga depends on.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Saga

## Saga -1: Repo scaffolding (COMPLETE)
Repo compartmentalization scaffolding.

## Saga 0: Foundation (COMPLETE)
Foundation and contracts.

## Saga 1: Dense tensor substrate v1 (COMPLETE)
Shape, DenseArray, reshape, transpose, indexing.

## Saga 2: Parser and evaluator foundation (COMPLETE)
Lexer, AST, parser with precedence, AST-walking evaluator.

## Saga 3: CLI and REPL v1 (COMPLETE)
Working REPL with all v1 syntax, built-in functions, tracing.

## Saga 4: Structured trace v1 (COMPLETE)
TraceEvent/Trace types, evaluator instrumentation, JSON export.

## Saga 5: Visual web viewer v1 (DEFERRED)
Deferred to post-MVP. MVP uses CLI + JSON trace export.

## MVP
Sagas 0 through 4 complete. MVP ships with REPL + trace export.

## Saga 6: ML foundations (COMPLETE)
Sigmoid, tanh_fn, pow, comparison operators, axis reductions, mean,
array constructors (zeros, ones, fill), and the first end-to-end
logistic regression demo. Delivered v0.2.

## Saga 7: SVG visualization v1 (COMPLETE)
`mlpl-viz` crate, `svg(data, type[, aux])` built-in, diagram types
(scatter, line, bar, heatmap, decision_boundary), `grid()` helper,
high-level analysis helpers (hist, scatter_labeled, loss_curve,
confusion_matrix, boundary_2d), browser REPL inline SVG rendering,
and download button. Delivered v0.3.

## Saga 10: Optimizers + training loop (COMPLETE)
Built proper training infrastructure on top of Saga 9 autograd.
New built-ins: `momentum_sgd(loss, params, lr, beta)` and
`adam(loss, params, lr, b1, b2, eps)` with per-parameter state
held in an `OptimizerState` map on the evaluation environment;
`cosine_schedule(step, total, lr_min, lr_max)` and
`linear_warmup(step, warmup, lr)` pure scalar schedules; and
two non-linear synthetic datasets `moons(seed, n, noise)` and
`circles(seed, n, noise)` returning `[N, 3]` `[x, y, label]`
matrices in the same layout as `blobs`. New `train N { body }`
language construct binds the iteration index to `step` inside
the body and captures each iteration's final value into a
`last_losses` 1-D array, replacing the manual
`repeat { grad; manual update; record loss }` recipe. Two new
demos -- `demos/moons_mlp.mlpl` and `demos/circles_mlp.mlpl` --
train a tanh MLP with `adam` inside `train { }` and render the
decision boundary with `boundary_2d`. New "Optimizers and
Schedules" tutorial lesson added to the web REPL. Delivered v0.6.

## Saga 9: Autograd v1 (COMPLETE)
Reverse-mode autograd as a language primitive. New `mlpl-autograd`
crate provides a tape-based `Tensor` with backward over add, sub,
mul, div, neg, exp, log, relu, tanh, sigmoid, softmax, sum, mean,
transpose, reshape, and matmul, all gradcheck-verified against
finite differences. Parser surface adds `param[shape]` and
`tensor[shape]` constructors, and a new `grad(expr, wrt)` built-in
lifts array expressions onto the tape and returns the gradient
with respect to a tracked parameter. The v0.4 `tiny_mlp` and
`softmax_classifier` demos were ported to use `param + grad`
instead of hand-written backprop, and a new "Automatic
Differentiation" tutorial lesson walks from a scalar minimization
to a one-layer linear regression. Delivered v0.5.

## Saga 8: ML demos (COMPLETE)
Synthetic data primitives (random, randn, argmax, blobs) and
higher-level ML built-ins (softmax, one_hot), plus six demos wired
into the browser REPL: k-means clustering, PCA via power iteration,
a linear softmax classifier, a tiny MLP on XOR-style data, and a
scaled dot-product attention pattern. Tutorial lessons added for
each. Delivered v0.4.

# MLPL Multi-Saga Plan

This plan sequences the remaining work from `docs/research2.txt`
(the small-LLM / training-platform vision) against the current
state of the repo. Completed sagas are listed in `docs/saga.md`;
through v0.4 we have the language core, REPL, SVG viz, ML
built-ins, and six end-to-end demos, but every demo still carries
its own hand-written gradients. Everything downstream of autograd
depends on removing that limitation.

## Guiding principles

- Each saga delivers a visible user-facing win (a demo or a
  workflow), not just plumbing.
- Preserve the "thinking space" loop: train -> visualize ->
  adjust. Visualization is core syntax, not an afterthought.
- Backends (MLX, CUDA) come only after the CPU path is correct,
  tested, and expressive enough to be worth accelerating.
- Keep the cellular crate layout; new capabilities land as new
  crates, not as bulges in existing ones.
- TDD + quality gates per CLAUDE.md on every step.

## Completed sagas (context)

- **Saga -1** Repo scaffolding
- **Saga 0** Foundation and contracts
- **Saga 1** Dense tensor substrate v1 (Shape, DenseArray, reshape, transpose, indexing)
- **Saga 2** Parser and evaluator foundation
- **Saga 3** CLI and REPL v1
- **Saga 4** Structured trace v1 (JSON export)
- **Saga 5** Visual web viewer v1 -- DEFERRED
- **Saga 6** ML foundations (v0.2): sigmoid, tanh, pow, comparisons,
  axis reductions, mean, zeros/ones/fill, logistic regression demo
- **Saga 7** SVG visualization v1 (v0.3): `mlpl-viz`, `svg()`,
  scatter/line/bar/heatmap/decision_boundary, hist, scatter_labeled,
  loss_curve, confusion_matrix, boundary_2d, browser REPL inline SVG
- **Saga 8** ML demos (v0.4): random/randn/argmax/blobs/softmax/one_hot;
  k-means, PCA, softmax classifier, tiny MLP, attention pattern demos;
  tutorial lessons for each
- **Saga 9** Autograd v1 (v0.5): `mlpl-autograd` reverse-mode tape;
  `param[...]` / `tensor[...]` constructors; `grad(expr, wrt)` built-in;
  tiny MLP and softmax classifier demos ported off hand-written backprop;
  "Automatic differentiation" tutorial lesson
- **Saga 10** Optimizers + training loop (v0.6): `momentum_sgd` and
  `adam` built-ins with per-param state on the environment;
  `cosine_schedule` / `linear_warmup` scalar helpers; `moons` and
  `circles` synthetic datasets; new `train N { body }` construct
  with implicit `step` binding and `last_losses` capture;
  `moons_mlp` and `circles_mlp` demos; "Optimizers and Schedules"
  tutorial lesson

## Future saga sequence

### Saga 11 -- Model DSL (NEXT)
`chain[...]`, `residual[...]`, `norm[...]`, `attention[...]`
combinators. Parameter trees with named addressing. Rebuild the
tiny MLP and attention demos using the DSL; add a 2-layer
transformer block demo (still CPU, still tiny).

### Saga 11.5 -- Named axes and shape introspection
Labeled shape metadata on `Value::Array`, annotation syntax on
assignment (`x : [batch, time, dim] = ...`), label propagation
through matmul/elementwise/reductions, structured `ShapeMismatch`
errors, and agent-facing introspection commands (`:describe`,
`:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`). Inserted between Saga 11 and Saga 12
because every later saga (Tiny LM, LoRA, attention variants,
embedding viz) benefits from labeled axes. Surface-only milestone:
no new kernels, no new backends, CPU only. See
`docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

### Saga 12 -- Tokenizers, datasets, and experiment tracking
Streaming/lazy dataset ops (`load`, `tokenize`, `shuffle`,
`batch`), a byte-level BPE tokenizer, and `experiment "name"`
objects with seed control, config snapshots, and logged metrics.
Reproducibility story lands here.

### Saga 13 -- Tiny LM end-to-end
Train a character-level or tiny-BPE language model (~1-5M params)
on a small corpus entirely in MLPL on CPU. Visualize loss,
sample generations, and attention maps. This is the first saga
that proves the platform thesis end-to-end.

### Saga 14 -- MLX backend
Backend abstraction layer (`backend <- mlx | cpu`), lazy
execution graph, kernel fusion for the hot ops. Re-run the tiny
LM on Apple Silicon and show a concrete speedup. CUDA deferred.

### Saga 15 -- LoRA / QLoRA and quantization
`lora[rank=8] model`, layer-scoped adapters, shared subspaces,
4-bit quantization for the frozen base. Fine-tune the Saga 13
model on a small instruction set.

### Saga 16 -- Embedding visualization and RAG
`embed`, `plot pca/tsne/umap`, 3D scatter via SVG projection,
nearest-neighbor links. RAG pipeline demo over a small corpus.
Builds directly on the existing viz stack.

### Saga 17 -- CUDA backend and distributed execution
CUDA kernels for the fused ops from Saga 14; `run model on
nodes[...]` primitives; device placement syntax. Homelab LAN
training demo.

### Saga 18 -- Distillation, ICL/ICRL, engram memory
Distillation pipelines, trajectory-based updates, engram-style
memory attachments, and multi-model orchestration
(`orchestrate[planner, executor, verifier]`). The research
frontier items from section 6 of `research2.txt`.

### Saga 19 -- LLM-as-tool integration
REST client built-ins, teacher-model distillation workflows,
codegen helpers. Intentionally last: secondary to the
"build your own model" story.

## Dependency graph (abbreviated)

```
9 autograd
  |-- 10 optimizers -- 11 model DSL -- 11.5 named axes -- 12 data+tracking -- 13 tiny LM
  |                                                                                |
  |                                                                                +-- 14 MLX
  |                                                                                +-- 15 LoRA
  |                                                                                +-- 16 viz/RAG
  |                                                                                +-- 17 CUDA/dist
  |                                                                                +-- 18 distill/ICRL
  |                                                                                +-- 19 LLM REST
```

Sagas 14-19 can reorder based on hardware access and interest;
9-13 (plus the inserted 11.5) must run in order.

## Start next: finish Saga 11, then Saga 11.5 -- Named axes

Saga 11 (Model DSL) is mostly shipped: model values, atomic and
composed layers, params walker, residual + rms_norm + attention,
differentiable `apply()` through the tape (Linear/Chain/Activation,
then also Residual/RmsNorm/single-head Attention as of step 007),
and ported MLP + transformer-block demos. Remaining Saga 11 work
is the tutorial lesson (done ahead of schedule on 2026-04-09) and
the v0.7.0 release.

After Saga 11 ships as v0.7.0, Saga 11.5 adds labeled shapes and
introspection -- the surface-only milestone surfaced by
`docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` as the single biggest agent-usability
gap. See `docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

+  `tiny_mlp` demos ported to one-line `chain(...)` form; new
-combinators. Parameter trees with named addressing. Rebuild the
-tiny MLP and attention demos using the DSL; add a 2-layer

  axis reductions, mean, zeros/ones/fill, logistic regression demo
- **Saga 7** SVG visualization v1 (v0.3): `mlpl-viz`, `svg()`,
  scatter/line/bar/heatmap/decision_boundary, hist, scatter_labeled,
  loss_curve, confusion_matrix, boundary_2d, browser REPL inline SVG
- **Saga 8** ML demos (v0.4): random/randn/argmax/blobs/softmax/one_hot;
  k-means, PCA, softmax classifier, tiny MLP, attention pattern demos;
  tutorial lessons for each
- **Saga 9** Autograd v1 (v0.5): `mlpl-autograd` reverse-mode tape;
  `param[...]` / `tensor[...]` constructors; `grad(expr, wrt)` built-in;
  tiny MLP and softmax classifier demos ported off hand-written backprop;
  "Automatic differentiation" tutorial lesson
- **Saga 10** Optimizers + training loop (v0.6): `momentum_sgd` and
  `adam` built-ins with per-param state on the environment;
  `cosine_schedule` / `linear_warmup` scalar helpers; `moons` and
  `circles` synthetic datasets; new `train N { body }` construct
  with implicit `step` binding and `last_losses` capture;
  `moons_mlp` and `circles_mlp` demos; "Optimizers and Schedules"
  tutorial lesson
- **Saga 11** Model DSL (v0.7): `Value::Model` runtime value;
  `linear(in, out, seed)` atomic layer; parameter-free
  `tanh_layer` / `relu_layer` / `softmax_layer` activations;
  `chain(...)` sequential composition; `residual(block)` skip
  connections; `rms_norm(dim)` normalization; `attention(
  d_model, heads, seed)` multi-head self-attention (tape-lowered
  for `heads=1`); `params(model)` walker and optimizer
  integration so `adam(loss, mdl, ...)` walks the parameter
  tree; differentiable `apply(mdl, X)`; `moons_mlp` and
  `tiny_mlp` demos ported to one-line `chain(...)` form; new
  `transformer_block.mlpl` demo (loss 143.87 -> 1.02 over 100
  Adam steps); `:vars` / `:models` / `:fns` / `:wsid` /
  `:describe` REPL introspection commands; "Model Composition"
  tutorial lesson. See `docs/milestone-modeldsl.md`.

## Future saga sequence

### Saga 11.5 -- Named axes and shape introspection (NEXT)
Labeled shape metadata on `Value::Array`, annotation syntax on
assignment (`x : [batch, time, dim] = ...`), label propagation
through matmul/elementwise/reductions, and structured
`ShapeMismatch` errors. Inserted between Saga 11 and Saga 12
because every later saga (Tiny LM, LoRA, attention variants,
embedding viz) benefits from labeled axes. Surface-only milestone:
no new kernels, no new backends, CPU only. The agent-facing
introspection commands (`:describe`, `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`)
already shipped as part of Saga 11. See
`docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

### Saga 12 -- Tokenizers, datasets, and experiment tracking
Streaming/lazy dataset ops (`load`, `tokenize`, `shuffle`,
`batch`), a byte-level BPE tokenizer, and `experiment "name"`
objects with seed control, config snapshots, and logged metrics.
Reproducibility story lands here.

### Saga 13 -- Tiny LM end-to-end
Train a character-level or tiny-BPE language model (~1-5M params)
on a small corpus entirely in MLPL on CPU. Visualize loss,
sample generations, and attention maps. This is the first saga
that proves the platform thesis end-to-end.

### Saga 14 -- MLX backend
Backend abstraction layer (`backend <- mlx | cpu`), lazy
execution graph, kernel fusion for the hot ops. Re-run the tiny
LM on Apple Silicon and show a concrete speedup. CUDA deferred.

### Saga 15 -- LoRA / QLoRA and quantization
`lora[rank=8] model`, layer-scoped adapters, shared subspaces,
4-bit quantization for the frozen base. Fine-tune the Saga 13
model on a small instruction set.

### Saga 16 -- Embedding visualization and RAG
`embed`, `plot pca/tsne/umap`, 3D scatter via SVG projection,
nearest-neighbor links. RAG pipeline demo over a small corpus.
Builds directly on the existing viz stack.

### Saga 17 -- CUDA backend and distributed execution
CUDA kernels for the fused ops from Saga 14; `run model on
nodes[...]` primitives; device placement syntax. Homelab LAN
training demo.

### Saga 18 -- Distillation, ICL/ICRL, engram memory
Distillation pipelines, trajectory-based updates, engram-style
memory attachments, and multi-model orchestration
(`orchestrate[planner, executor, verifier]`). The research
frontier items from section 6 of `research2.txt`.

### Saga 19 -- LLM-as-tool integration
REST client built-ins, teacher-model distillation workflows,
codegen helpers. Intentionally last: secondary to the
"build your own model" story.

## Dependency graph (abbreviated)

```
9 autograd
  |-- 10 optimizers -- 11 model DSL -- 11.5 named axes -- 12 data+tracking -- 13 tiny LM
  |                                                                                |
  |                                                                                +-- 14 MLX
  |                                                                                +-- 15 LoRA
  |                                                                                +-- 16 viz/RAG
  |                                                                                +-- 17 CUDA/dist
  |                                                                                +-- 18 distill/ICRL
  |                                                                                +-- 19 LLM REST
```

Sagas 14-19 can reorder based on hardware access and interest;
9-13 (plus the inserted 11.5) must run in order.

## Start next: finish Saga 11, then Saga 11.5 -- Named axes

Saga 11 (Model DSL) is mostly shipped: model values, atomic and
composed layers, params walker, residual + rms_norm + attention,
differentiable `apply()` through the tape (Linear/Chain/Activation,
then also Residual/RmsNorm/single-head Attention as of step 007),
and ported MLP + transformer-block demos. Remaining Saga 11 work
is the tutorial lesson (done ahead of schedule on 2026-04-09) and
the v0.7.0 release.

After Saga 11 ships as v0.7.0, Saga 11.5 adds labeled shapes and
introspection -- the surface-only milestone surfaced by
`docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` as the single biggest agent-usability
gap. See `docs/milestone-named-axes.md` for the 10-step plan.

content.content:
1	# Are We Driven Yet?
2	
3	**Purpose.** Track MLPL's progress against the two forcing-function
4	documents that drive its long-range design:
5	
6	- `docs/paper-driven-development.txt` -- a curated backlog of ~20
7	  recent ML papers (Dec 2025 - Apr 2026) and the MLPL primitives
8	  each one would exercise.
9	- `docs/ai-agent-driven-development.txt` -- a wishlist from the
10	  perspective of an LLM/agent that wants to use MLPL as its
11	  reasoning and execution substrate.
12	
13	Both documents are speculative discussions, not plans of record.
14	This file is the accounting sheet that turns them into actionable
15	status: for every capability they call out, does MLPL already have
16	it, is it planned in `docs/plan.md`, or does it still need to be
17	shaped into a saga?
18	
19	## Legend
20	
21	| Symbol | Meaning |
22	|--------|---------|
23	| `HAVE` | Shipped, exercised by demos/tests today |
24	| `PART` | Partially implemented, needs deepening to satisfy the source doc |
25	| `PLAN` | Already on the Saga 12-19 roadmap in `docs/plan.md` |
26	| `CONS` | Not planned; needs design + a saga shape before it can move |
27	
28	**Source** column: `P` = from `paper-driven-development.txt`, `A` =
29	from `ai-agent-driven-development.txt`, `P+A` = both.
30	
31	---
32	
33	## 1. Core language and array semantics
34	
35	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
36	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
37	| Dense tensors with row-major storage | A | HAVE | `mlpl-array` |
38	| Scalar broadcasting | A | HAVE | Scalar op array across all primitives |
39	| Element-wise arithmetic and reductions | A | HAVE | `reduce_add`/`reduce_mul` with axis arg |
40	| Matrix multiplication, transpose | A | HAVE | `matmul`, `transpose`, `dot` |
41	| Shape introspection (`shape`, `rank`) | A | HAVE | Built-ins |
42	| Repeat loop and `train N { }` sugar | P+A | HAVE | Saga 10 |
43	| Small core language (few, composable primitives) | A | HAVE | Intentional design constraint |
44	| Canonical formatting of MLPL source | A | CONS | No MLPL-level formatter exists; Rust side uses rustfmt. Needed for LLM roundtripping |
45	| REPL with incremental execution | A | HAVE | `mlpl-repl` + web REPL |
46	| Named axes on tensors (`x[batch, time, dim]`) | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 (`docs/milestone-named-axes.md`) |
47	| Shape-checked / dependent-shape types (`Tensor[B, T, D]`) | A | PART | Saga 11.5 tracks labels as metadata with runtime validation, stopping short of full dependent types |
48	| Dual syntax: terse APL mode vs explicit agent-friendly mode | A | CONS | Open design question |
49	| Code-as-data: `parse`, `transform`, `eval` primitives | A | CONS | Needed for meta-programming, self-modification |
50	| Deterministic execution mode (`with deterministic { }`) | A | CONS | Seed control needs a scoped construct |
51	| Deterministic serialization for programs + models | A | CONS | Needed for repeatable agent loops |
52	| Notebook mode with persisted cell state | A | CONS | Web REPL has session state but no cell model |
53	
54	## 2. Autograd, optimizers, training
55	
56	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
57	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
58	| Reverse-mode autograd (`grad(expr, wrt)`) | A | HAVE | Saga 9 |
59	| `param[shape]` trainable-leaf constructor | A | HAVE | Saga 9 |
60	| `momentum_sgd`, `adam` with persistent state | P+A | HAVE | Saga 10 |
61	| `cosine_schedule`, `linear_warmup` helpers | P | HAVE | Saga 10 |
62	| Model DSL (`linear`/`chain`/activations) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 11, training through `apply()` wired 2026-04-09 |
63	| Model DSL: `residual` / `rms_norm` / `attention` forward | P | HAVE | Saga 11 steps 004-005 |
64	| Model DSL: differentiable `apply()` through residual/norm/attention | P | PART | Forward-only today; tape lowering TODO before Saga 11 step 007 |
65	| `jacobian f`, `vmap f` | A | CONS | Not on the Saga 11 roadmap; Jax-style transforms would be a Saga 11.5 item |
66	| Named-parameter trees and parameter addressing | P | PART | `ModelSpec::params()` returns a flat list; no path-addressed subtree ops |
67	| Hyperparameter sweep DSL (adapter/rank/lr grid) | P | PLAN | Saga 12 ("experiment tracking") |
68	| Experiment registry + comparison plots | P | PLAN | Saga 12 |
69	| Hessian / eigenvalue / curvature probes | P | CONS | Paper #5 (Learning Rate Matters) asks for this; no plan |
70	| LoRA / QLoRA adapter declarations | P | PLAN | Saga 15 |
71	| 4-bit / low-precision quantization | P | PLAN | Saga 15 |
72	| Self-distillation pipeline primitives (sample -> filter -> finetune) | P | CONS | Papers #17-18; not on roadmap |
73	| RLVR / token-level credit assignment | P | CONS | Paper #17; not on roadmap |
74	
75	## 3. Datasets and data pipelines
76	
77	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
78	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
79	| Synthetic datasets (`moons`, `blobs`, `circles`, `randn`) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 8/10 |
80	| `load.csv`, generic file loaders | A | PLAN | Saga 12 |
81	| Streaming / lazy dataset ops (`shuffle`, `batch`, `map`) | A | PLAN | Saga 12 |
82	| Byte-level BPE tokenizer | P | PLAN | Saga 12 |
83	| Long-context / 100M-token loader | P | CONS | Paper #13 (MSA); needs backend work |
84	| External memory store (key-value table, writeback, sparse addressing) | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("engram memory") covers the idea; no concrete API |
85	
86	## 4. Visualization, tracing, demos
87	
88	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
89	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
90	| Inline SVG visualization (scatter/line/bar/heatmap) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 7 |
91	| Analysis helpers (`hist`, `scatter_labeled`, `loss_curve`, `confusion_matrix`, `boundary_2d`) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 8 |
92	| Execution trace with JSON export | A | HAVE | `mlpl-trace` |
93	| Yew/WASM web REPL | P+A | HAVE | Saga 5 |
94	| Tutorial mode in web REPL | A | HAVE | 19 lessons, missing a Model DSL one |
95	| Numeric output summarization (collapsible stats) | A | HAVE | Added 2026-04-09 |
96	| Embedding viz: PCA / t-SNE / UMAP projections | P+A | PLAN | Saga 16 |
97	| 3D scatter via SVG projection, nearest-neighbor links | P | PLAN | Saga 16 |
98	| Topology / block-diagram view of model graphs | P | CONS | Paper #2 (mHC) wants it; needs a `ModelSpec` -> SVG pass |
99	| Debate / "society of thought" transcript viewer | P | CONS | Paper #19; not on roadmap |
100	| Skill graph + reuse frequency chart | P | CONS | Paper #10; not on roadmap |
101	| Kernel performance leaderboard + diff view | P | CONS | Paper #12 (CUDA Agent); not on roadmap |
102	| PaperBanana-style slide generation from a run | P | CONS | Artifact generator; not on roadmap |
103	
104	## 5. Backends and runtime integrations
105	
106	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
107	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
108	| CPU dense kernels (current default) | A | HAVE | `mlpl-array` |
109	| Explicit device placement syntax (`x \|> cuda`, `with mlx { }`) | A | CONS | Required by both source docs; no construct chosen yet |
110	| MLX backend | P+A | PLAN | Saga 14 |
111	| Lazy execution graph + kernel fusion | P | PLAN | Saga 14 |
112	| CUDA backend | P+A | PLAN | Saga 17 |
113	| Distributed execution (`run model on nodes[...]`) | P | PLAN | Saga 17 |
114	| Ollama / llama.cpp unified model interface | P+A | PLAN | Saga 19 (REST client built-ins) |
115	| Streaming `generate` with incremental tokens | A | CONS | Saga 19 covers REST; streaming as a language construct is not pinned down |
116	| Profiler ingestion / FLOPs estimation | P+A | CONS | Paper #12 wants it as a reward signal; no plan |
117	
118	## 6. Agent-facing ergonomics
119	
120	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
121	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
122	| Introspectable execution graph (`trace f`) | A | HAVE | `mlpl-trace` JSON export |
123	| Structured errors (kind + expected/actual fields) | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 phase 4 introduces a structured `ShapeMismatch` error variant and a JSON error channel |
124	| Built-in testing primitives (`assert`, `approx_equal`, `fuzz`) | A | CONS | Rust tests exist, MLPL-level asserts do not |
125	| Sandbox execution with time/memory/GPU limits (`run_safe`) | A | CONS | Needed for agent self-correction loops; no plan |
126	| Cost / performance introspection (`profile f x`) | A | CONS | Paper #12 also benefits; no plan |
127	| `:describe <name>` / `:vars` / `:fns` REPL introspection | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 phase 5 ports APL-style `)FNS`/`)VARS` conventions to the REPL |
128	| `explain f` structured-explanation primitive | A | CONS | Speculative but high-leverage for agent tooling |
129	| `repair f given error` primitive | A | CONS | Speculative; depends on structured errors + code-as-data |
130	| `optimize f for gpu` primitive | A | CONS | Speculative; depends on device placement + profile |
131	| Tool schemas and tool-call execution | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("ICL/ICRL") + Saga 19 |
132	| Trajectory / episode / rollout / reward records | P | PLAN | Saga 18 |
133	| Controller/worker multi-model orchestration | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("multi-model orchestration") |
134	| Replay buffers and curriculum control | P | PLAN | Saga 18 |
135	
136	## 7. Paper-specific primitives (the 20-paper backlog)
137	
138	Papers from `docs/paper-driven-development.txt` ranked backlog. Each
139	row is "what MLPL would need to make this paper a one-screen demo."
140	
141	| # | Paper | MLPL primitive asked for | Status |
142	|---|-------|--------------------------|--------|
143	| 1 | Recursive Language Models | Recursive task/decompose/reduce constructs; scheduler for nested subcalls with budget + memoization | CONS |
144	| 2 | mHC: Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections | First-class model-graph syntax for residual-stream variants and constrained projection ops | PART (ModelSpec exists, constrained projections do not) |
145	| 3 | Conditional Memory via Scalable Lookup | Memory tables, lookup, writeback, sparse addressing as first-class ops | PLAN (Saga 18) |
146	| 4 | In-Context RL for Tool Use | `episode`, `rollout`, `reward`, `tool`, fewshot curriculum syntax | PLAN (Saga 18) |
147	| 5 | Learning Rate Matters (LoRA tuning) | Sweep DSL (`adapter`, `rank`, `lr`, `scheduler`, `seed`, `judge`) | PLAN (Saga 12 + 15) |
148	| 6 | RelayLLM | Controller/worker decoding, token-level help requests, cooperative generation | PLAN (Saga 18 + 19) |
149	| 7 | CM2 Checklist Rewards | Declarative checklist reward specs with evidence anchors | CONS |
150	| 8 | PEARL planner/executor | Separate `plan { }` and `execute { }` stages with distinct rewards | CONS |
151	| 9 | Tool-R0 self-evolving agents | Self-play / dual-agent curriculum loops | CONS |
152	| 10 | SkillRL | `skill` as a reusable packaged abstraction (code + prompt + tests + permissions) | CONS |
153	| 11 | Agent World Model | Environment DSL for executable synthetic worlds and tools | CONS |
154	| 12 | CUDA Agent | Kernel blocks, verification hooks, profiler-aware reward terms | PART-PLAN (Saga 17 provides CUDA; codegen loop is unplanned) |
155	| 13 | MSA (100M-token attention) | Long-context memory/attention operators; memory-interleaving | CONS |
156	| 14 | Self-Indexing KVCache | Cache-policy syntax, programmable inference-time memory layout | CONS |
157	| 15 | S3-Attention | `retrieve` from attention primitives blurring retrieval + inference | CONS |
158	| 16 | iGRPO self-feedback | `draft`, `select_best`, `refine` syntax for two-stage loops | CONS |
159	| 17 | Self-Distilled RLVR | Token-level credit assignment and distilled-target generation | CONS |
160	| 18 | Self-distillation for code | `sample -> filter -> finetune` pipeline primitives | CONS |
161	| 19 | Societies of Thought | `voices`, `debate`, `reconcile`, `critic`, `judge` multi-perspective syntax | CONS |
162	| 20 | Attention Residuals | Residual-policy syntax; skip/accumulate as first-class choice | PART (ModelSpec has `residual`; no policy selection) |
163	
164	---
165	
166	## Aggregate scoreboard
167	
168	| Bucket | Count | What it means |
169	|--------|-------|---------------|
170	| HAVE   | ~25   | Shipped, exercised by demos/tests |
171	| PART   | ~6    | Partially there; needs deepening (notably differentiable `apply` through residual/norm/attention, named-parameter trees, structured errors, mHC architecture graph) |
172	| PLAN   | ~18   | On the Sagas 12-19 roadmap |
173	| CONS   | ~30   | Not on the roadmap; each is a candidate for a new saga or an inserted step |
174	
175	## What this tells us about priorities
176	
177	Historical note (2026-04-09): the three clusters originally listed
178	here were (1) named axes + shape types, (2) structured errors +
179	trace-driven self-correction, and (3) differentiable `apply`
180	through residual/rms_norm/attention. Cluster 3 was shipped the
181	same day as Saga 11 steps 006 and 007. Clusters 1 and 2 were
182	folded into a new **Saga 11.5 -- Named axes and shape
183	introspection** (`docs/milestone-named-axes.md`), inserted between
184	Saga 11 and Saga 12.
185	
186	With the top three addressed, the remaining uncovered region is
187	the rest of the **agent-as-user** half: device
188	placement, profiling, sandboxing, deterministic mode, code-as-data,
189	testing, notebook mode, canonical formatting. None of these fit
190	naturally into Sagas 12-19 as currently scoped. A dedicated
191	"Saga 11.6: Agent ergonomics" milestone would be a reasonable home
192	for the smaller ones; the bigger ones (code-as-data, notebook) are
193	probably Saga-sized on their own.
194	
195	On the **paper-driven** side, the 20-paper backlog has four
196	genuine design questions that no current saga covers:
197	
198	- Recursive task orchestration (paper #1)
199	- Skill packaging (paper #10)
200	- Environment DSL (paper #11)
201	- Checklist / rubric-composed rewards (papers #7, #8)
202	
203	These are not blockers for any existing saga but they collectively
204	justify reordering Saga 18 to land sooner, or splitting it into two
205	sagas along the "primitives first, orchestration second" seam.
206	
207	## How to use this file
208	
209	- Before opening a new saga, check the CONS table and see if there
210	  is a capability that blocks it.
211	- Before adding a paper demo, confirm the row in section 7 has
212	  moved out of CONS, or accept that the demo will inline the
213	  missing primitive.
214	- Update this file whenever a capability crosses buckets. Keep it
215	  honest -- the point is to know which side (papers or agents) is
216	  pulling MLPL forward and which side is falling behind.
