# Are We Driven Yet?

**Purpose.** Track MLPL's progress against the two forcing-function
documents that drive its long-range design:

- `docs/paper-driven-development.txt` -- a curated backlog of ~20
  recent ML papers (Dec 2025 - Apr 2026) and the MLPL primitives
  each one would exercise.
- `docs/ai-agent-driven-development.txt` -- a wishlist from the
  perspective of an LLM/agent that wants to use MLPL as its
  reasoning and execution substrate.

Both documents are speculative discussions, not plans of record.
This file is the accounting sheet that turns them into actionable
status: for every capability they call out, does MLPL already have
it, is it planned in `docs/plan.md`, or does it still need to be
shaped into a saga?

## Legend

| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `HAVE` | Shipped, exercised by demos/tests today |
| `PART` | Partially implemented, needs deepening to satisfy the source doc |
| `PLAN` | Already on the Saga 12-19 roadmap in `docs/plan.md` |
| `CONS` | Not planned; needs design + a saga shape before it can move |

**Source** column: `P` = from `paper-driven-development.txt`, `A` =
from `ai-agent-driven-development.txt`, `P+A` = both.

---

## 1. Core language and array semantics

| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|--------|-------|
| Dense tensors with row-major storage | A | HAVE | `mlpl-array` |
| Scalar broadcasting | A | HAVE | Scalar op array across all primitives |
| Element-wise arithmetic and reductions | A | HAVE | `reduce_add`/`reduce_mul` with axis arg |
| Matrix multiplication, transpose | A | HAVE | `matmul`, `transpose`, `dot` |
| Shape introspection (`shape`, `rank`) | A | HAVE | Built-ins |
| Repeat loop and `train N { }` sugar | P+A | HAVE | Saga 10 |
| Small core language (few, composable primitives) | A | HAVE | Intentional design constraint |
| Canonical formatting of MLPL source | A | CONS | No MLPL-level formatter exists; Rust side uses rustfmt. Needed for LLM roundtripping |
| REPL with incremental execution | A | HAVE | `mlpl-repl` + web REPL |
| Named axes on tensors (`x[batch, time, dim]`) | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 (`docs/milestone-named-axes.md`) |
| Shape-checked / dependent-shape types (`Tensor[B, T, D]`) | A | PART | Saga 11.5 tracks labels as metadata with runtime validation, stopping short of full dependent types |
| Dual syntax: terse APL mode vs explicit agent-friendly mode | A | CONS | Open design question |
| Code-as-data: `parse`, `transform`, `eval` primitives | A | CONS | Needed for meta-programming, self-modification |
| Deterministic execution mode (`with deterministic { }`) | A | CONS | Seed control needs a scoped construct |
| Deterministic serialization for programs + models | A | CONS | Needed for repeatable agent loops |
| Notebook mode with persisted cell state | A | CONS | Web REPL has session state but no cell model |

## 2. Autograd, optimizers, training

| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|--------|-------|
| Reverse-mode autograd (`grad(expr, wrt)`) | A | HAVE | Saga 9 |
| `param[shape]` trainable-leaf constructor | A | HAVE | Saga 9 |
| `momentum_sgd`, `adam` with persistent state | P+A | HAVE | Saga 10 |
| `cosine_schedule`, `linear_warmup` helpers | P | HAVE | Saga 10 |
| Model DSL (`linear`/`chain`/activations) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 11, training through `apply()` wired 2026-04-09 |
| Model DSL: `residual` / `rms_norm` / `attention` forward | P | HAVE | Saga 11 steps 004-005 |
| Model DSL: differentiable `apply()` through residual/norm/attention | P | PART | Forward-only today; tape lowering TODO before Saga 11 step 007 |
| `jacobian f`, `vmap f` | A | CONS | Not on the Saga 11 roadmap; Jax-style transforms would be a Saga 11.5 item |
| Named-parameter trees and parameter addressing | P | PART | `ModelSpec::params()` returns a flat list; no path-addressed subtree ops |
| Hyperparameter sweep DSL (adapter/rank/lr grid) | P | PLAN | Saga 12 ("experiment tracking") |
| Experiment registry + comparison plots | P | PLAN | Saga 12 |
| Hessian / eigenvalue / curvature probes | P | CONS | Paper #5 (Learning Rate Matters) asks for this; no plan |
| LoRA / QLoRA adapter declarations | P | PLAN | Saga 15 |
| 4-bit / low-precision quantization | P | PLAN | Saga 15 |
| Self-distillation pipeline primitives (sample -> filter -> finetune) | P | CONS | Papers #17-18; not on roadmap |
| RLVR / token-level credit assignment | P | CONS | Paper #17; not on roadmap |

## 3. Datasets and data pipelines

| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|--------|-------|
| Synthetic datasets (`moons`, `blobs`, `circles`, `randn`) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 8/10 |
| `load.csv`, generic file loaders | A | PLAN | Saga 12 |
| Streaming / lazy dataset ops (`shuffle`, `batch`, `map`) | A | PLAN | Saga 12 |
| Byte-level BPE tokenizer | P | PLAN | Saga 12 |
| Long-context / 100M-token loader | P | CONS | Paper #13 (MSA); needs backend work |
| External memory store (key-value table, writeback, sparse addressing) | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("engram memory") covers the idea; no concrete API |

## 4. Visualization, tracing, demos

| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|--------|-------|
| Inline SVG visualization (scatter/line/bar/heatmap) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 7 |
| Analysis helpers (`hist`, `scatter_labeled`, `loss_curve`, `confusion_matrix`, `boundary_2d`) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 8 |
| Execution trace with JSON export | A | HAVE | `mlpl-trace` |
| Yew/WASM web REPL | P+A | HAVE | Saga 5 |
| Tutorial mode in web REPL | A | HAVE | 19 lessons, missing a Model DSL one |
| Numeric output summarization (collapsible stats) | A | HAVE | Added 2026-04-09 |
| Embedding viz: PCA / t-SNE / UMAP projections | P+A | PLAN | Saga 16 |
| 3D scatter via SVG projection, nearest-neighbor links | P | PLAN | Saga 16 |
| Topology / block-diagram view of model graphs | P | CONS | Paper #2 (mHC) wants it; needs a `ModelSpec` -> SVG pass |
| Debate / "society of thought" transcript viewer | P | CONS | Paper #19; not on roadmap |
| Skill graph + reuse frequency chart | P | CONS | Paper #10; not on roadmap |
| Kernel performance leaderboard + diff view | P | CONS | Paper #12 (CUDA Agent); not on roadmap |
| PaperBanana-style slide generation from a run | P | CONS | Artifact generator; not on roadmap |

## 5. Backends and runtime integrations

| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|--------|-------|
| CPU dense kernels (current default) | A | HAVE | `mlpl-array` |
| Explicit device placement syntax (`x \|> cuda`, `with mlx { }`) | A | CONS | Required by both source docs; no construct chosen yet |
| MLX backend | P+A | PLAN | Saga 14 |
| Lazy execution graph + kernel fusion | P | PLAN | Saga 14 |
| CUDA backend | P+A | PLAN | Saga 17 |
| Distributed execution (`run model on nodes[...]`) | P | PLAN | Saga 17 |
| Ollama / llama.cpp unified model interface | P+A | PLAN | Saga 19 (REST client built-ins) |
| Streaming `generate` with incremental tokens | A | CONS | Saga 19 covers REST; streaming as a language construct is not pinned down |
| Profiler ingestion / FLOPs estimation | P+A | CONS | Paper #12 wants it as a reward signal; no plan |

## 6. Agent-facing ergonomics

| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|--------|-------|
| Introspectable execution graph (`trace f`) | A | HAVE | `mlpl-trace` JSON export |
| Structured errors (kind + expected/actual fields) | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 phase 4 introduces a structured `ShapeMismatch` error variant and a JSON error channel |
| Built-in testing primitives (`assert`, `approx_equal`, `fuzz`) | A | CONS | Rust tests exist, MLPL-level asserts do not |
| Sandbox execution with time/memory/GPU limits (`run_safe`) | A | CONS | Needed for agent self-correction loops; no plan |
| Cost / performance introspection (`profile f x`) | A | CONS | Paper #12 also benefits; no plan |
| `:describe <name>` / `:vars` / `:fns` REPL introspection | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 phase 5 ports APL-style `)FNS`/`)VARS` conventions to the REPL |
| `explain f` structured-explanation primitive | A | CONS | Speculative but high-leverage for agent tooling |
| `repair f given error` primitive | A | CONS | Speculative; depends on structured errors + code-as-data |
| `optimize f for gpu` primitive | A | CONS | Speculative; depends on device placement + profile |
| Tool schemas and tool-call execution | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("ICL/ICRL") + Saga 19 |
| Trajectory / episode / rollout / reward records | P | PLAN | Saga 18 |
| Controller/worker multi-model orchestration | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("multi-model orchestration") |
| Replay buffers and curriculum control | P | PLAN | Saga 18 |

## 7. Paper-specific primitives (the 20-paper backlog)

Papers from `docs/paper-driven-development.txt` ranked backlog. Each
row is "what MLPL would need to make this paper a one-screen demo."

| # | Paper | MLPL primitive asked for | Status |
|---|-------|--------------------------|--------|
| 1 | Recursive Language Models | Recursive task/decompose/reduce constructs; scheduler for nested subcalls with budget + memoization | CONS |
| 2 | mHC: Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections | First-class model-graph syntax for residual-stream variants and constrained projection ops | PART (ModelSpec exists, constrained projections do not) |
| 3 | Conditional Memory via Scalable Lookup | Memory tables, lookup, writeback, sparse addressing as first-class ops | PLAN (Saga 18) |
| 4 | In-Context RL for Tool Use | `episode`, `rollout`, `reward`, `tool`, fewshot curriculum syntax | PLAN (Saga 18) |
| 5 | Learning Rate Matters (LoRA tuning) | Sweep DSL (`adapter`, `rank`, `lr`, `scheduler`, `seed`, `judge`) | PLAN (Saga 12 + 15) |
| 6 | RelayLLM | Controller/worker decoding, token-level help requests, cooperative generation | PLAN (Saga 18 + 19) |
| 7 | CM2 Checklist Rewards | Declarative checklist reward specs with evidence anchors | CONS |
| 8 | PEARL planner/executor | Separate `plan { }` and `execute { }` stages with distinct rewards | CONS |
| 9 | Tool-R0 self-evolving agents | Self-play / dual-agent curriculum loops | CONS |
| 10 | SkillRL | `skill` as a reusable packaged abstraction (code + prompt + tests + permissions) | CONS |
| 11 | Agent World Model | Environment DSL for executable synthetic worlds and tools | CONS |
| 12 | CUDA Agent | Kernel blocks, verification hooks, profiler-aware reward terms | PART-PLAN (Saga 17 provides CUDA; codegen loop is unplanned) |
| 13 | MSA (100M-token attention) | Long-context memory/attention operators; memory-interleaving | CONS |
| 14 | Self-Indexing KVCache | Cache-policy syntax, programmable inference-time memory layout | CONS |
| 15 | S3-Attention | `retrieve` from attention primitives blurring retrieval + inference | CONS |
| 16 | iGRPO self-feedback | `draft`, `select_best`, `refine` syntax for two-stage loops | CONS |
| 17 | Self-Distilled RLVR | Token-level credit assignment and distilled-target generation | CONS |
| 18 | Self-distillation for code | `sample -> filter -> finetune` pipeline primitives | CONS |
| 19 | Societies of Thought | `voices`, `debate`, `reconcile`, `critic`, `judge` multi-perspective syntax | CONS |
| 20 | Attention Residuals | Residual-policy syntax; skip/accumulate as first-class choice | PART (ModelSpec has `residual`; no policy selection) |

---

## Aggregate scoreboard

| Bucket | Count | What it means |
|--------|-------|---------------|
| HAVE   | ~25   | Shipped, exercised by demos/tests |
| PART   | ~6    | Partially there; needs deepening (notably differentiable `apply` through residual/norm/attention, named-parameter trees, structured errors, mHC architecture graph) |
| PLAN   | ~18   | On the Sagas 12-19 roadmap |
| CONS   | ~30   | Not on the roadmap; each is a candidate for a new saga or an inserted step |

## What this tells us about priorities

Historical note (2026-04-09): the three clusters originally listed
here were (1) named axes + shape types, (2) structured errors +
trace-driven self-correction, and (3) differentiable `apply`
through residual/rms_norm/attention. Cluster 3 was shipped the
same day as Saga 11 steps 006 and 007. Clusters 1 and 2 were
folded into a new **Saga 11.5 -- Named axes and shape
introspection** (`docs/milestone-named-axes.md`), inserted between
Saga 11 and Saga 12.

With the top three addressed, the remaining uncovered region is
the rest of the **agent-as-user** half: device
placement, profiling, sandboxing, deterministic mode, code-as-data,
testing, notebook mode, canonical formatting. None of these fit
naturally into Sagas 12-19 as currently scoped. A dedicated
"Saga 11.6: Agent ergonomics" milestone would be a reasonable home
for the smaller ones; the bigger ones (code-as-data, notebook) are
probably Saga-sized on their own.

On the **paper-driven** side, the 20-paper backlog has four
genuine design questions that no current saga covers:

- Recursive task orchestration (paper #1)
- Skill packaging (paper #10)
- Environment DSL (paper #11)
- Checklist / rubric-composed rewards (papers #7, #8)

These are not blockers for any existing saga but they collectively
justify reordering Saga 18 to land sooner, or splitting it into two
sagas along the "primitives first, orchestration second" seam.

## How to use this file

- Before opening a new saga, check the CONS table and see if there
  is a capability that blocks it.
- Before adding a paper demo, confirm the row in section 7 has
  moved out of CONS, or accept that the demo will inline the
  missing primitive.
- Update this file whenever a capability crosses buckets. Keep it
  honest -- the point is to know which side (papers or agents) is
  pulling MLPL forward and which side is falling behind.

1	# Named Axes and Shape Introspection Milestone (Saga 11.5, v0.7.5)
2	
3	## Why this exists
4	
5	`docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` flagged named-axes and shape-checking as
6	the single highest-leverage "agent usability" gap in the
7	`docs/ai-agent-driven-development.txt` wishlist. It is also the
8	common pain point behind half the shape errors you hit writing demos
9	by hand: once an expression has four matmul-reshape-transpose steps,
10	tracking which axis is which becomes the hard part.
11	
12	Positional indexing is opaque for both humans and agents:
13	
14	```mlpl
15	scores = matmul(Q, transpose(K)) / sqrt(4)   # which axis is which?
16	```
17	
18	Named indexing is self-documenting:
19	
20	```mlpl
21	Q : [seq, d_k]
22	K : [seq, d_k]
23	scores : [seq, seq] = matmul(Q, K.T["seq", "d_k" -> "d_k", "seq"])
24	```
25	
26	Saga 11.5 is a *surface-only* milestone: no new kernels, no new
27	backends, no new ML primitives. It teaches MLPL to carry axis labels
28	and shape metadata through the evaluator so error messages are
29	actionable and agents can reason about tensor validity before they
30	run code.
31	
32	This saga is inserted between Saga 11 (Model DSL) and Saga 12
33	(Tokenizers/datasets) because every later saga -- Tiny LM, LoRA,
34	attention variants, embedding viz -- benefits from labeled axes
35	and would otherwise accumulate the same hand-written shape-juggling
36	that the Model DSL just deleted.
37	
38	## Non-goals
39	
40	- Full dependent types. Shape labels are metadata, not a type
41	  system; mismatches are runtime errors with rich context, not
42	  compile-time rejection. A future saga can tighten this.
43	- Changes to `DenseArray` row-major storage. Labels live on
44	  `Value::Array` wrappers, not inside the array buffer.
45	- New tensor ops. Every existing op keeps its current semantics;
46	  it just learns to propagate and validate labels.
47	- Backend work. CPU only.
48	- Tokenizers, datasets, experiment tracking. That is still Saga 12.
49	
50	## Quality requirements (every step)
51	
52	Identical to Saga 11:
53	
54	1. TDD: failing test first, then implementation, then refactor.
55	2. Quality gates must all pass before commit:
56	   - `cargo test`
57	   - `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
58	   - `cargo fmt --all` + `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
59	   - `markdown-checker -f "**/*.md"` if docs touched
60	   - `sw-checklist`
61	3. Use `/mw-cp` checkpoint process.
62	4. Push immediately after commit.
63	5. Web UI changes rebuild `pages/` via `scripts/build-pages.sh`.
64	
65	## What already exists
66	
67	- `mlpl_array::Shape` with `dims()` and `rank()` accessors.
68	- `Value::Array` / `Value::Model` / `Value::Str` enum in
69	  `mlpl-eval`. Labels would ride on `Value::Array`.
70	- Structured `EvalError` enum per crate, used everywhere errors
71	  surface (good substrate for shape-mismatch improvements).
72	- `:help` REPL command and web help dialog (good surface to
73	  extend with `:describe <name>`).
74	
75	## Phases
76	
77	### Phase 1: Labeled value wrapper
78	
79	Introduce an axis-labeled shape and thread it through the array
80	value type without changing any existing behavior.
81	
82	- New type `LabeledShape { dims: Vec<usize>, labels: Vec<Option<String>> }`
83	  in `mlpl-array` or `mlpl-core`. `None` labels mean "positional"
84	  so pre-existing code continues to work unchanged.
85	- `Value::Array` gains an optional labels field (or wraps in a
86	  small struct). Default construction leaves labels `None`.
87	- `shape(x)` continues to return the raw dim vector. New
88	  `labels(x)` built-in returns a string vector of the axis names
89	  (empty string for positional axes).
90	- Zero behavior change until labels are introduced by Phase 2.
91	
92	### Phase 2: Label introduction primitives
93	
94	Give users a way to attach labels.
95	
96	- `label(x, ["batch", "time", "dim"])` tags an existing array.
97	- Annotation syntax on assignment: `x : [batch, time, dim] = randn(7, [8, 16, 32])`.
98	  The annotation is optional; omitted means positional.
99	- `x.T` / `transpose(x)` preserves labels but swaps their order.
100	- `reshape(x, ...)` clears labels by default (reshape loses
101	  semantic axis identity) with a `reshape_labeled(x, new_shape, new_labels)`
102	  opt-in for explicit re-labeling.
103	
104	### Phase 3: Label-aware ops
105	
106	Make the existing op set propagate labels through:
107	
108	- Elementwise (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`): require matching labels when
109	  both operands are labeled; union in the usual broadcasting order.
110	- `matmul(a, b)`: require the contraction axis label to match
111	  (e.g. `[seq, d] @ [d, heads] -> [seq, heads]`).
112	- `reduce_add(x, axis)` and friends accept either an integer axis
113	  or an axis name: `reduce_add(x, "time")`.
114	- `softmax(x, "d_k")` and `softmax(x, 1)` both work.
115	
116	At each op, label mismatches raise a structured `EvalError::ShapeMismatch`
117	with the operator name, expected labels, and actual labels so that
118	both humans and agents can repair the error without re-running.
119	
120	### Phase 4: Structured shape errors
121	
122	Replace the string `EvalError::Unsupported(..)` shape errors with a
123	dedicated variant:
124	
125	- `EvalError::ShapeMismatch { op, expected: LabeledShape, actual: LabeledShape }`
126	  rendered as a rich REPL message (with colors in the web UI) and
127	  as a JSON object when an environment flag is set.
128	- The web REPL's error formatter learns the new variant and shows
129	  a one-line "expected ... got ... at axis ..." summary with the
130	  offending op name.
131	- The existing error site in `mlpl-eval::eval_ops` for broadcasting
132	  failures migrates to the new variant.
133	
134	### Phase 5: Agent-facing introspection
135	
136	Wire the label metadata into the existing `:help` surface:
137	
138	- New `:describe <name>` REPL command: prints the variable's
139	  labeled shape, dtype, and the first couple of slices. For models
140	  it prints the `ModelSpec` tree with per-layer labeled shapes on
141	  the input/output.
142	- New `:vars` / `:models` / `:fns` REPL commands (scope-local
143	  versions of APL's `)VARS` and `)FNS`), documented in the web
144	  help dialog.
145	- `trace` JSON export includes axis labels so downstream tooling
146	  can reason about shape flow through a run.
147	
148	### Phase 6: Docs, tutorial, release
149	
150	- New tutorial lesson "Named Axes" immediately after "Matrices"
151	  but before "Linear Algebra", so every subsequent lesson can use
152	  labels.
153	- Update the Model DSL lesson and the moons/transformer demos to
154	  label their inputs and outputs. The Model DSL itself should
155	  report `[seq, d_model]` inputs and outputs on `apply(mdl, X)`.
156	- Update `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md`: move Named Axes,
157	  Shape-Checked Arrays, Structured Errors, and `:describe` from
158	  CONS to PLAN/HAVE.
159	- Update `docs/plan.md` to mark Saga 11.5 COMPLETE.
160	- Bump REPL banners to v0.7.5.
161	- Rebuild and deploy `pages/`.
162	- Tag `v0.7.5-named-axes`.
163	
164	## Planned steps
165	
166	| # | Slug | Phase | What it delivers |
167	|---|------|-------|------------------|
168	| 001 | labeled-shape-type | 1 | `LabeledShape` + `labels()` built-in; no behavior change |
169	| 002 | label-introduction | 2 | `label(x, [...])`, annotation syntax on assignment |
170	| 003 | label-reshape-rules | 2 | transpose preserves, reshape clears, `reshape_labeled` added |
171	| 004 | label-propagation-elementwise | 3 | +, -, *, / propagate and validate labels |
172	| 005 | label-propagation-matmul-reduce | 3 | matmul, reduce_add, softmax by axis name |

file.content:
# Named Axes and Shape Introspection Milestone (Saga 11.5, v0.7.5)

## Why this exists

`docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` flagged named-axes and shape-checking as
the single highest-leverage "agent usability" gap in the
`docs/ai-agent-driven-development.txt` wishlist. It is also the
common pain point behind half the shape errors you hit writing demos
by hand: once an expression has four matmul-reshape-transpose steps,
tracking which axis is which becomes the hard part.

Positional indexing is opaque for both humans and agents:

```mlpl
scores = matmul(Q, transpose(K)) / sqrt(4)   # which axis is which?
```

Named indexing is self-documenting:

```mlpl
Q : [seq, d_k]
K : [seq, d_k]
scores : [seq, seq] = matmul(Q, K.T["seq", "d_k" -> "d_k", "seq"])
```

Saga 11.5 is a *surface-only* milestone: no new kernels, no new
backends, no new ML primitives. It teaches MLPL to carry axis labels
and shape metadata through the evaluator so error messages are
actionable and agents can reason about tensor validity before they
run code.

This saga is inserted between Saga 11 (Model DSL) and Saga 12
(Tokenizers/datasets) because every later saga -- Tiny LM, LoRA,
attention variants, embedding viz -- benefits from labeled axes
and would otherwise accumulate the same hand-written shape-juggling
that the Model DSL just deleted.

## Non-goals

- Full dependent types. Shape labels are metadata, not a type
  system; mismatches are runtime errors with rich context, not
  compile-time rejection. A future saga can tighten this.
- Changes to `DenseArray` row-major storage. Labels live on
  `Value::Array` wrappers, not inside the array buffer.
- New tensor ops. Every existing op keeps its current semantics;
  it just learns to propagate and validate labels.
- Backend work. CPU only.
- Tokenizers, datasets, experiment tracking. That is still Saga 12.

## Quality requirements (every step)

Identical to Saga 11:

1. TDD: failing test first, then implementation, then refactor.
2. Quality gates must all pass before commit:
   - `cargo test`
   - `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
   - `cargo fmt --all` + `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
   - `markdown-checker -f "**/*.md"` if docs touched
   - `sw-checklist`
3. Use `/mw-cp` checkpoint process.
4. Push immediately after commit.
5. Web UI changes rebuild `pages/` via `scripts/build-pages.sh`.

## What already exists

- `mlpl_array::Shape` with `dims()` and `rank()` accessors.
- `Value::Array` / `Value::Model` / `Value::Str` enum in
  `mlpl-eval`. Labels would ride on `Value::Array`.
- Structured `EvalError` enum per crate, used everywhere errors
  surface (good substrate for shape-mismatch improvements).
- `:help` REPL command and web help dialog (good surface to
  extend with `:describe <name>`).

## Phases

### Phase 1: Labeled value wrapper

Introduce an axis-labeled shape and thread it through the array
value type without changing any existing behavior.

- New type `LabeledShape { dims: Vec<usize>, labels: Vec<Option<String>> }`
  in `mlpl-array` or `mlpl-core`. `None` labels mean "positional"
  so pre-existing code continues to work unchanged.
- `Value::Array` gains an optional labels field (or wraps in a
  small struct). Default construction leaves labels `None`.
- `shape(x)` continues to return the raw dim vector. New
  `labels(x)` built-in returns a string vector of the axis names
  (empty string for positional axes).
- Zero behavior change until labels are introduced by Phase 2.

### Phase 2: Label introduction primitives

Give users a way to attach labels.

- `label(x, ["batch", "time", "dim"])` tags an existing array.
- Annotation syntax on assignment: `x : [batch, time, dim] = randn(7, [8, 16, 32])`.
  The annotation is optional; omitted means positional.
- `x.T` / `transpose(x)` preserves labels but swaps their order.
- `reshape(x, ...)` clears labels by default (reshape loses
  semantic axis identity) with a `reshape_labeled(x, new_shape, new_labels)`
  opt-in for explicit re-labeling.

### Phase 3: Label-aware ops

Make the existing op set propagate labels through:

- Elementwise (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`): require matching labels when
  both operands are labeled; union in the usual broadcasting order.
- `matmul(a, b)`: require the contraction axis label to match
  (e.g. `[seq, d] @ [d, heads] -> [seq, heads]`).
- `reduce_add(x, axis)` and friends accept either an integer axis
  or an axis name: `reduce_add(x, "time")`.
- `softmax(x, "d_k")` and `softmax(x, 1)` both work.

At each op, label mismatches raise a structured `EvalError::ShapeMismatch`
with the operator name, expected labels, and actual labels so that
both humans and agents can repair the error without re-running.

### Phase 4: Structured shape errors

Replace the string `EvalError::Unsupported(..)` shape errors with a
dedicated variant:

- `EvalError::ShapeMismatch { op, expected: LabeledShape, actual: LabeledShape }`
  rendered as a rich REPL message (with colors in the web UI) and
  as a JSON object when an environment flag is set.
- The web REPL's error formatter learns the new variant and shows
  a one-line "expected ... got ... at axis ..." summary with the
  offending op name.
- The existing error site in `mlpl-eval::eval_ops` for broadcasting
  failures migrates to the new variant.

### Phase 5: Agent-facing introspection

Wire the label metadata into the existing `:help` surface:

- New `:describe <name>` REPL command: prints the variable's
  labeled shape, dtype, and the first couple of slices. For models
  it prints the `ModelSpec` tree with per-layer labeled shapes on
  the input/output.
- New `:vars` / `:models` / `:fns` REPL commands (scope-local
  versions of APL's `)VARS` and `)FNS`), documented in the web
  help dialog.
- `trace` JSON export includes axis labels so downstream tooling
  can reason about shape flow through a run.

### Phase 6: Docs, tutorial, release

- New tutorial lesson "Named Axes" immediately after "Matrices"
  but before "Linear Algebra", so every subsequent lesson can use
  labels.
- Update the Model DSL lesson and the moons/transformer demos to
  label their inputs and outputs. The Model DSL itself should
  report `[seq, d_model]` inputs and outputs on `apply(mdl, X)`.
- Update `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md`: move Named Axes,
  Shape-Checked Arrays, Structured Errors, and `:describe` from
  CONS to PLAN/HAVE.
- Update `docs/plan.md` to mark Saga 11.5 COMPLETE.
- Bump REPL banners to v0.7.5.
- Rebuild and deploy `pages/`.
- Tag `v0.7.5-named-axes`.

## Planned steps

| # | Slug | Phase | What it delivers |
|---|------|-------|------------------|
| 001 | labeled-shape-type | 1 | `LabeledShape` + `labels()` built-in; no behavior change |
| 002 | label-introduction | 2 | `label(x, [...])`, annotation syntax on assignment |
| 003 | label-reshape-rules | 2 | transpose preserves, reshape clears, `reshape_labeled` added |
| 004 | label-propagation-elementwise | 3 | +, -, *, / propagate and validate labels |
| 005 | label-propagation-matmul-reduce | 3 | matmul, reduce_add, softmax by axis name |
| 006 | shape-mismatch-error | 4 | Structured `ShapeMismatch` error variant + rendering |
| 007 | describe-vars-fns-commands | 5 | `:describe`, `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns` REPL commands |
| 008 | trace-json-labels | 5 | Label metadata in trace JSON export |
| 009 | named-axes-tutorial-lesson | 6 | New lesson + Model DSL lesson refresh |
| 010 | named-axes-release-v075 | 6 | docs, banners, release tag |

Ten steps. Expect some merging once implementation starts --
steps 004 and 005 in particular may collapse if the elementwise
path generalizes cleanly.

## Success criteria

- A labeled array prints its labels alongside its shape in the
  REPL: `X: [seq=6, d_model=4]`.
- A deliberately-wrong matmul (e.g. `matmul(A_labeled[batch, dim],
  B_labeled[time, dim])`) raises a structured `ShapeMismatch`
  error naming the op, the expected contraction axis, and the
  actual labels.
- `:describe mdl` on a stored model prints its layer tree with
  the expected input and output labeled shapes for the first
  layer.
- `:vars` lists every bound array with its labeled shape.
- Every existing demo still runs unchanged: positional arrays
  continue to work without labels.
- `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` shows at least four rows moving
  from CONS to HAVE.
- All quality gates green; pages deployed; release tagged.

## Risks and open questions

- **Label propagation through broadcasting.** Scalar broadcast is
  easy. Row-vector-against-matrix broadcast is fine if only one
  side is labeled. Two labeled sides with compatible dims but
  different labels is genuinely ambiguous -- should it error, or
  silently prefer the left-hand labels? Default proposal: error,
  with an explicit `relabel(x, [...])` escape hatch.
- **Parser impact of the annotation syntax.** `x : [a, b, c] = ...`
  introduces a colon in assignment that the current parser does
  not expect. Step 002 may require a small parser addition; keep
  it contained so the rest of the grammar is unaffected.
- **`ModelSpec` labeling.** Models are defined before they see any
  input, so their input labels are unknown at construction time.
  Proposal: track labels opportunistically -- the first `apply`
  call pins them, and subsequent `apply` calls with mismatched
  labels raise a structured error. Alternative: require models
  to be constructed with an explicit signature. Decide in step 005.
- **Rank-3+ tensors.** The current op surface is largely rank-2.
  Labels for rank-3+ are trivial metadata-wise, but per-head
  attention (multi-head, currently blocked on slicing) would
  benefit from labeled rank-3 paths. Out of scope for this saga;
  note it as a hand-off to a later attention-slicing saga.

content.text:
Here's the state of play.

## Recent docs status

**Stale in `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md`** (last touched 2026-04-09, before Saga 11 finished):
- Row 64 "differentiable `apply()` through residual/norm/attention" — still says **PART** / "tape lowering TODO before step 007". Now **HAVE** for single-head (commit fb90fb4).
- Row 94 "Tutorial mode in web REPL" — note says "19 lessons, missing a Model DSL one". Now has it (commit 934d60d).
- Row 127 "`:describe` / `:vars` / `:fns`" — still says **PLAN / Saga 11.5 phase 5**. Already shipped in Saga 11 (commits 99f7c8a, 623652d).

**Stale in `docs/milestone-named-axes.md`**:
- Step 007 `describe-vars-fns-commands` — already shipped. Saga 11.5 is effectively a **9-step plan** now, not 10.
- Phase 5 needs to drop the `:describe/:vars/:fns` bullet and keep only `trace JSON labels`.

`docs/missing-demos.md` and `docs/milestone-modeldsl.md` are current. `docs/milestone-named-axes.md` otherwise still reads well.

## Archive a completed saga — yes

`.agentrail/saga.toml` shows `status = "completed"` for `mlpl-modeldsl`. The archive pattern from `.agentrail-archive/mlpl-optim-20260408T155006/` is `mv .agentrail .agentrail-archive/<name>-<UTC-timestamp>` then re-init a fresh `.agentrail/` for the next saga. This is exactly how Saga 10 handed off to Saga 11.

## Goals that can be scheduled next

Three candidates, in priority order:

1. **Saga 11.5 — Named Axes & Shape Introspection** (already planned, `docs/milestone-named-axes.md`). Pruned to ~9 steps since `:describe/:vars/:fns` already shipped. Surface-only, CPU-only, biggest agent-usability lever per `are-we-driven-yet.md`. **Recommended — the roadmap already points here.**
2. **Saga 11.6 — Agent Ergonomics** (suggested but unplanned). Would collect the CONS-bucket items from sections 1 + 6: deterministic mode, sandboxed `run_safe`, `profile f`, `assert`/`approx_equal`, canonical MLPL formatter, notebook cell model. Smaller items; could be done as a sibling to 11.5 or merged into it.
3. **Saga 12 — Tokenizers/Datasets/Experiment Tracking** (already planned). Larger. Unblocks Saga 13 (Tiny LM). Should wait until 11.5 lands so datasets carry labeled axes.

Want me to (a) fix the stale rows in `are-we-driven-yet.md` + `milestone-named-axes.md`, (b) archive `mlpl-modeldsl` and init `mlpl-named-axes`, or (c) both?

Workflow CLI for keeping AI agents on track

Usage: agentrail [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  init            Initialize a new saga
  setup           Bootstrap a project: create saga, CLAUDE.md, and register domain
  add             Add a step to the saga (for maintenance mode or ad-hoc tasks)
  status          Show current saga state
  next            Output current step prompt and context for a fresh agent session
  begin           Mark the current step as in-progress
  complete        Complete current step, optionally define next step
  plan            View or update the saga plan
  history         Show all step summaries
  distill         Distill trajectories into a skill document
  run-loop        Auto-execute deterministic steps, pause at agent steps
  abort           Mark current step as blocked
  archive         Archive current saga to .agentrail-archive/ and clear .agentrail/
  audit           Compare git history with saga history, report gaps
  snapshot        Save a point-in-time copy of .agentrail/ into the git object store
  gen-agents-doc  Write AGENTS.example.md — an agent instructions template for agentrail
  help            Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --saga <SAGA>  Path to the project directory (default: current directory) [default: .]
  -h, --help         Print help
  -V, --version      Print version

# Named Axes and Shape Introspection Milestone (Saga 11.5, v0.7.5)

## Why this exists

`docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` flagged named-axes and shape-checking as
the single highest-leverage "agent usability" gap in the
`docs/ai-agent-driven-development.txt` wishlist. It is also the
common pain point behind half the shape errors you hit writing demos
by hand: once an expression has four matmul-reshape-transpose steps,
tracking which axis is which becomes the hard part.

Positional indexing is opaque for both humans and agents:

```mlpl
scores = matmul(Q, transpose(K)) / sqrt(4)   # which axis is which?
```

Named indexing is self-documenting:

```mlpl
Q : [seq, d_k]
K : [seq, d_k]
scores : [seq, seq] = matmul(Q, K.T["seq", "d_k" -> "d_k", "seq"])
```

Saga 11.5 is a *surface-only* milestone: no new kernels, no new
backends, no new ML primitives. It teaches MLPL to carry axis labels
and shape metadata through the evaluator so error messages are
actionable and agents can reason about tensor validity before they
run code.

This saga is inserted between Saga 11 (Model DSL) and Saga 12
(Tokenizers/datasets) because every later saga -- Tiny LM, LoRA,
attention variants, embedding viz -- benefits from labeled axes
and would otherwise accumulate the same hand-written shape-juggling
that the Model DSL just deleted.

## Non-goals

- Full dependent types. Shape labels are metadata, not a type
  system; mismatches are runtime errors with rich context, not
  compile-time rejection. A future saga can tighten this.
- Changes to `DenseArray` row-major storage. Labels live on
  `Value::Array` wrappers, not inside the array buffer.
- New tensor ops. Every existing op keeps its current semantics;
  it just learns to propagate and validate labels.
- Backend work. CPU only.
- Tokenizers, datasets, experiment tracking. That is still Saga 12.

## Quality requirements (every step)

Identical to Saga 11:

1. TDD: failing test first, then implementation, then refactor.
2. Quality gates must all pass before commit:
   - `cargo test`
   - `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
   - `cargo fmt --all` + `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
   - `markdown-checker -f "**/*.md"` if docs touched
   - `sw-checklist`
3. Use `/mw-cp` checkpoint process.
4. Push immediately after commit.
5. Web UI changes rebuild `pages/` via `scripts/build-pages.sh`.

## What already exists

- `mlpl_array::Shape` with `dims()` and `rank()` accessors.
- `Value::Array` / `Value::Model` / `Value::Str` enum in
  `mlpl-eval`. Labels would ride on `Value::Array`.
- Structured `EvalError` enum per crate, used everywhere errors
  surface (good substrate for shape-mismatch improvements).
- `:help` REPL command and web help dialog (good surface to
  extend with `:describe <name>`).

## Phases

### Phase 1: Labeled value wrapper

Introduce an axis-labeled shape and thread it through the array
value type without changing any existing behavior.

- New type `LabeledShape { dims: Vec<usize>, labels: Vec<Option<String>> }`
  in `mlpl-array` or `mlpl-core`. `None` labels mean "positional"
  so pre-existing code continues to work unchanged.
- `Value::Array` gains an optional labels field (or wraps in a
  small struct). Default construction leaves labels `None`.
- `shape(x)` continues to return the raw dim vector. New
  `labels(x)` built-in returns a string vector of the axis names
  (empty string for positional axes).
- Zero behavior change until labels are introduced by Phase 2.

### Phase 2: Label introduction primitives

Give users a way to attach labels.

- `label(x, ["batch", "time", "dim"])` tags an existing array.
- Annotation syntax on assignment: `x : [batch, time, dim] = randn(7, [8, 16, 32])`.
  The annotation is optional; omitted means positional.
- `x.T` / `transpose(x)` preserves labels but swaps their order.
- `reshape(x, ...)` clears labels by default (reshape loses
  semantic axis identity) with a `reshape_labeled(x, new_shape, new_labels)`
  opt-in for explicit re-labeling.

### Phase 3: Label-aware ops

Make the existing op set propagate labels through:

- Elementwise (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`): require matching labels when
  both operands are labeled; union in the usual broadcasting order.
- `matmul(a, b)`: require the contraction axis label to match
  (e.g. `[seq, d] @ [d, heads] -> [seq, heads]`).
- `reduce_add(x, axis)` and friends accept either an integer axis
  or an axis name: `reduce_add(x, "time")`.
- `softmax(x, "d_k")` and `softmax(x, 1)` both work.

At each op, label mismatches raise a structured `EvalError::ShapeMismatch`
with the operator name, expected labels, and actual labels so that
both humans and agents can repair the error without re-running.

### Phase 4: Structured shape errors

Replace the string `EvalError::Unsupported(..)` shape errors with a
dedicated variant:

- `EvalError::ShapeMismatch { op, expected: LabeledShape, actual: LabeledShape }`
  rendered as a rich REPL message (with colors in the web UI) and
  as a JSON object when an environment flag is set.
- The web REPL's error formatter learns the new variant and shows
  a one-line "expected ... got ... at axis ..." summary with the
  offending op name.
- The existing error site in `mlpl-eval::eval_ops` for broadcasting
  failures migrates to the new variant.

### Phase 5: Agent-facing introspection

Wire the label metadata into the existing `:help` surface:

- New `:describe <name>` REPL command: prints the variable's
  labeled shape, dtype, and the first couple of slices. For models
  it prints the `ModelSpec` tree with per-layer labeled shapes on
  the input/output.
- New `:vars` / `:models` / `:fns` REPL commands (scope-local
  versions of APL's `)VARS` and `)FNS`), documented in the web
  help dialog.
- `trace` JSON export includes axis labels so downstream tooling
  can reason about shape flow through a run.

### Phase 6: Docs, tutorial, release

- New tutorial lesson "Named Axes" immediately after "Matrices"
  but before "Linear Algebra", so every subsequent lesson can use
  labels.
- Update the Model DSL lesson and the moons/transformer demos to
  label their inputs and outputs. The Model DSL itself should
  report `[seq, d_model]` inputs and outputs on `apply(mdl, X)`.
- Update `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md`: move Named Axes,
  Shape-Checked Arrays, Structured Errors, and `:describe` from
  CONS to PLAN/HAVE.
- Update `docs/plan.md` to mark Saga 11.5 COMPLETE.
- Bump REPL banners to v0.7.5.
- Rebuild and deploy `pages/`.
- Tag `v0.7.5-named-axes`.

## Planned steps

| # | Slug | Phase | What it delivers |
|---|------|-------|------------------|
| 001 | labeled-shape-type | 1 | `LabeledShape` + `labels()` built-in; no behavior change |
| 002 | label-introduction | 2 | `label(x, [...])`, annotation syntax on assignment |
| 003 | label-reshape-rules | 2 | transpose preserves, reshape clears, `reshape_labeled` added |
| 004 | label-propagation-elementwise | 3 | +, -, *, / propagate and validate labels |
| 005 | label-propagation-matmul-reduce | 3 | matmul, reduce_add, softmax by axis name |
| 006 | shape-mismatch-error | 4 | Structured `ShapeMismatch` error variant + rendering |
| 007 | describe-vars-fns-commands | 5 | `:describe`, `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns` REPL commands |
| 008 | trace-json-labels | 5 | Label metadata in trace JSON export |
| 009 | named-axes-tutorial-lesson | 6 | New lesson + Model DSL lesson refresh |
| 010 | named-axes-release-v075 | 6 | docs, banners, release tag |

Ten steps. Expect some merging once implementation starts --
steps 004 and 005 in particular may collapse if the elementwise
path generalizes cleanly.

## Success criteria

- A labeled array prints its labels alongside its shape in the
  REPL: `X: [seq=6, d_model=4]`.
- A deliberately-wrong matmul (e.g. `matmul(A_labeled[batch, dim],
  B_labeled[time, dim])`) raises a structured `ShapeMismatch`
  error naming the op, the expected contraction axis, and the
  actual labels.
- `:describe mdl` on a stored model prints its layer tree with
  the expected input and output labeled shapes for the first
  layer.
- `:vars` lists every bound array with its labeled shape.
- Every existing demo still runs unchanged: positional arrays
  continue to work without labels.
- `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` shows at least four rows moving
  from CONS to HAVE.
- All quality gates green; pages deployed; release tagged.

## Risks and open questions

- **Label propagation through broadcasting.** Scalar broadcast is
  easy. Row-vector-against-matrix broadcast is fine if only one
  side is labeled. Two labeled sides with compatible dims but
  different labels is genuinely ambiguous -- should it error, or
  silently prefer the left-hand labels? Default proposal: error,
  with an explicit `relabel(x, [...])` escape hatch.
- **Parser impact of the annotation syntax.** `x : [a, b, c] = ...`
  introduces a colon in assignment that the current parser does
  not expect. Step 002 may require a small parser addition; keep
  it contained so the rest of the grammar is unaffected.
- **`ModelSpec` labeling.** Models are defined before they see any
  input, so their input labels are unknown at construction time.
  Proposal: track labels opportunistically -- the first `apply`
  call pins them, and subsequent `apply` calls with mismatched
  labels raise a structured error. Alternative: require models
  to be constructed with an explicit signature. Decide in step 005.
- **Rank-3+ tensors.** The current op surface is largely rank-2.
  Labels for rank-3+ are trivial metadata-wise, but per-head
  attention (multi-head, currently blocked on slicing) would
  benefit from labeled rank-3 paths. Out of scope for this saga;
  note it as a hand-off to a later attention-slicing saga.

+phase teaches them about labels and threads labels through the

can reason about shape flow through a run.


```mlpl

K : [seq, d_k]

```

Identical to Saga 11:

1. TDD: failing test first, then implementation, then refactor.
2. Quality gates must all pass before commit:
   - `cargo test`
   - `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
   - `cargo fmt --all` + `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
   - `markdown-checker -f "**/*.md"` if docs touched
   - `sw-checklist`
3. Use `/mw-cp` checkpoint process.
4. Push immediately after commit.
5. Web UI changes rebuild `pages/` via `scripts/build-pages.sh`.

## What already exists

- `mlpl_array::Shape` with `dims()` and `rank()` accessors.
- `Value::Array` / `Value::Model` / `Value::Str` enum in
  `mlpl-eval`. Labels would ride on `Value::Array`.
- Structured `EvalError` enum per crate, used everywhere errors
  surface (good substrate for shape-mismatch improvements).
- `:help` REPL command and web help dialog (good surface to
  extend with `:describe <name>`).

## Phases

### Phase 1: Labeled value wrapper

Introduce an axis-labeled shape and thread it through the array
value type without changing any existing behavior.

- New type `LabeledShape { dims: Vec<usize>, labels: Vec<Option<String>> }`
  in `mlpl-array` or `mlpl-core`. `None` labels mean "positional"
  so pre-existing code continues to work unchanged.
- `Value::Array` gains an optional labels field (or wraps in a
  small struct). Default construction leaves labels `None`.
- `shape(x)` continues to return the raw dim vector. New
  `labels(x)` built-in returns a string vector of the axis names
  (empty string for positional axes).
- Zero behavior change until labels are introduced by Phase 2.

### Phase 2: Label introduction primitives

Give users a way to attach labels.

- `label(x, ["batch", "time", "dim"])` tags an existing array.
- Annotation syntax on assignment: `x : [batch, time, dim] = randn(7, [8, 16, 32])`.
  The annotation is optional; omitted means positional.
- `x.T` / `transpose(x)` preserves labels but swaps their order.
- `reshape(x, ...)` clears labels by default (reshape loses
  semantic axis identity) with a `reshape_labeled(x, new_shape, new_labels)`
  opt-in for explicit re-labeling.

### Phase 3: Label-aware ops

Make the existing op set propagate labels through:

- Elementwise (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`): require matching labels when
  both operands are labeled; union in the usual broadcasting order.
- `matmul(a, b)`: require the contraction axis label to match
  (e.g. `[seq, d] @ [d, heads] -> [seq, heads]`).
- `reduce_add(x, axis)` and friends accept either an integer axis
  or an axis name: `reduce_add(x, "time")`.
- `softmax(x, "d_k")` and `softmax(x, 1)` both work.

At each op, label mismatches raise a structured `EvalError::ShapeMismatch`
with the operator name, expected labels, and actual labels so that
both humans and agents can repair the error without re-running.

### Phase 4: Structured shape errors

Replace the string `EvalError::Unsupported(..)` shape errors with a
dedicated variant:

- `EvalError::ShapeMismatch { op, expected: LabeledShape, actual: LabeledShape }`
  rendered as a rich REPL message (with colors in the web UI) and
  as a JSON object when an environment flag is set.
- The web REPL's error formatter learns the new variant and shows
  a one-line "expected ... got ... at axis ..." summary with the
  offending op name.
- The existing error site in `mlpl-eval::eval_ops` for broadcasting
  failures migrates to the new variant.

### Phase 5: Label-aware introspection and trace

The REPL introspection commands `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`,
`:wsid`, and `:describe` already shipped as part of Saga 11. This
phase teaches them about labels and threads labels through the
trace surface:

- `:describe <name>` learns to print a variable's labeled shape
  alongside its raw dims. For models it prints the `ModelSpec`
  tree with per-layer labeled shapes on the input/output, pinned
  from the first `apply` call.
- `:vars` output includes labeled shape for every labeled array.
- `trace` JSON export includes axis labels so downstream tooling
  can reason about shape flow through a run.

### Phase 6: Docs, tutorial, release

- New tutorial lesson "Named Axes" immediately after "Matrices"
  but before "Linear Algebra", so every subsequent lesson can use
  labels.
- Update the Model DSL lesson and the moons/transformer demos to
  label their inputs and outputs. The Model DSL itself should
  report `[seq, d_model]` inputs and outputs on `apply(mdl, X)`.
- Update `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md`: move Named Axes,
  Shape-Checked Arrays, Structured Errors, and `:describe` from
  CONS to PLAN/HAVE.
- Update `docs/plan.md` to mark Saga 11.5 COMPLETE.
- Bump REPL banners to v0.7.5.
- Rebuild and deploy `pages/`.
- Tag `v0.7.5-named-axes`.

## Planned steps

| # | Slug | Phase | What it delivers |
|---|------|-------|------------------|
| 001 | labeled-shape-type | 1 | `LabeledShape` + `labels()` built-in; no behavior change |
| 002 | label-introduction | 2 | `label(x, [...])`, annotation syntax on assignment |
| 003 | label-reshape-rules | 2 | transpose preserves, reshape clears, `reshape_labeled` added |
| 004 | label-propagation-elementwise | 3 | +, -, *, / propagate and validate labels |
| 005 | label-propagation-matmul-reduce | 3 | matmul, reduce_add, softmax by axis name |
| 006 | shape-mismatch-error | 4 | Structured `ShapeMismatch` error variant + rendering |
| 007 | describe-vars-fns-commands | 5 | `:describe`, `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns` REPL commands |
| 008 | trace-json-labels | 5 | Label metadata in trace JSON export |
| 009 | named-axes-tutorial-lesson | 6 | New lesson + Model DSL lesson refresh |
| 010 | named-axes-release-v075 | 6 | docs, banners, release tag |

Ten steps. Expect some merging once implementation starts --
steps 004 and 005 in particular may collapse if the elementwise
path generalizes cleanly.

## Success criteria

- A labeled array prints its labels alongside its shape in the
  REPL: `X: [seq=6, d_model=4]`.
- A deliberately-wrong matmul (e.g. `matmul(A_labeled[batch, dim],
  B_labeled[time, dim])`) raises a structured `ShapeMismatch`
  error naming the op, the expected contraction axis, and the
  actual labels.
- `:describe mdl` on a stored model prints its layer tree with
  the expected input and output labeled shapes for the first
  layer.
- `:vars` lists every bound array with its labeled shape.
- Every existing demo still runs unchanged: positional arrays
  continue to work without labels.
- `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` shows at least four rows moving
  from CONS to HAVE.
- All quality gates green; pages deployed; release tagged.

## Risks and open questions

- **Label propagation through broadcasting.** Scalar broadcast is
  easy. Row-vector-against-matrix broadcast is fine if only one
  side is labeled. Two labeled sides with compatible dims but
  different labels is genuinely ambiguous -- should it error, or
  silently prefer the left-hand labels? Default proposal: error,
  with an explicit `relabel(x, [...])` escape hatch.
- **Parser impact of the annotation syntax.** `x : [a, b, c] = ...`
  introduces a colon in assignment that the current parser does
  not expect. Step 002 may require a small parser addition; keep
  it contained so the rest of the grammar is unaffected.
- **`ModelSpec` labeling.** Models are defined before they see any
  input, so their input labels are unknown at construction time.
  Proposal: track labels opportunistically -- the first `apply`
  call pins them, and subsequent `apply` calls with mismatched
  labels raise a structured error. Alternative: require models
  to be constructed with an explicit signature. Decide in step 005.
- **Rank-3+ tensors.** The current op surface is largely rank-2.
  Labels for rank-3+ are trivial metadata-wise, but per-head
  attention (multi-head, currently blocked on slicing) would
  benefit from labeled rank-3 paths. Out of scope for this saga;
  note it as a hand-off to a later attention-slicing saga.

# Named Axes and Shape Introspection Milestone (Saga 11.5, v0.7.5)

## Why this exists

`docs/are-we-driven-yet.md` flagged named-axes and shape-checking as
the single highest-leverage "agent usability" gap in the
`docs/ai-agent-driven-development.txt` wishlist. It is also the
common pain point behind half the shape errors you hit writing demos
by hand: once an expression has four matmul-reshape-transpose steps,
tracking which axis is which becomes the hard part.

Positional indexing is opaque for both humans and agents:

```mlpl
scores = matmul(Q, transpose(K)) / sqrt(4)   # which axis is which?
```

Named indexing is self-documenting:

```mlpl
Q : [seq, d_k]
K : [seq, d_k]
scores : [seq, seq] = matmul(Q, K.T["seq", "d_k" -> "d_k", "seq"])
```

Saga 11.5 is a *surface-only* milestone: no new kernels, no new
backends, no new ML primitives. It teaches MLPL to carry axis labels
and shape metadata through the evaluator so error messages are
actionable and agents can reason about tensor validity before they
run code.

This saga is inserted between Saga 11 (Model DSL) and Saga 12
(Tokenizers/datasets) because every later saga -- Tiny LM, LoRA,
attention variants, embedding viz -- benefits from labeled axes
and would otherwise accumulate the same hand-written shape-juggling
that the Model DSL just deleted.

## Non-goals

- Full dependent types. Shape labels are metadata, not a type
  system; mismatches are runtime errors with rich context, not
  compile-time rejection. A future saga can tighten this.
- Changes to `DenseArray` row-major storage. Labels live on
  `Value::Array` wrappers, not inside the array buffer.
- New tensor ops. Every existing op keeps its current semantics;
  it just learns to propagate and validate labels.
- Backend work. CPU only.
- Tokenizers, datasets, experiment tracking. That is still Saga 12.

## Quality requirements (every step)

Identical to Saga 11:

1. TDD: failing test first, then implementation, then refactor.
2. Quality gates must all pass before commit:
   - `cargo test`
   - `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
   - `cargo fmt --all` + `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
   - `markdown-checker -f "**/*.md"` if docs touched
   - `sw-checklist`
3. Use `/mw-cp` checkpoint process.
4. Push immediately after commit.
5. Web UI changes rebuild `pages/` via `scripts/build-pages.sh`.

## What already exists

- `mlpl_array::Shape` with `dims()` and `rank()` accessors.
- `Value::Array` / `Value::Model` / `Value::Str` enum in
  `mlpl-eval`. Labels would ride on `Value::Array`.
- Structured `EvalError` enum per crate, used everywhere errors
  surface (good substrate for shape-mismatch improvements).
- `:help` REPL command and web help dialog (good surface to
  extend with `:describe <name>`).

## Phases

### Phase 1: Labeled value wrapper

Introduce an axis-labeled shape and thread it through the array
value type without changing any existing behavior.

- `Value::Array` gains an optional labels field (or wraps in a
  small struct). Default construction leaves labels `None`.
- `shape(x)` continues to return the raw dim vector. New
  `labels(x)` built-in returns a string vector of the axis names
  (empty string for positional axes).
- Zero behavior change until labels are introduced by Phase 2.

### Phase 2: Label introduction primitives

Give users a way to attach labels.

- `label(x, ["batch", "time", "dim"])` tags an existing array.

  The annotation is optional; omitted means positional.
- `x.T` / `transpose(x)` preserves labels but swaps their order.
- `reshape(x, ...)` clears labels by default (reshape loses
  semantic axis identity) with a `reshape_labeled(x, new_shape, new_labels)`
  opt-in for explicit re-labeling.

### Phase 3: Label-aware ops

Make the existing op set propagate labels through:

  both operands are labeled; union in the usual broadcasting order.
- `matmul(a, b)`: require the contraction axis label to match

- `reduce_add(x, axis)` and friends accept either an integer axis

- `softmax(x, "d_k")` and `softmax(x, 1)` both work.

At each op, label mismatches raise a structured `EvalError::ShapeMismatch`

### Phase 4: Structured shape errors

- New tutorial lesson "Named Axes" immediately after "Matrices"

  labels.
- Update the Model DSL lesson and the moons/transformer demos to

  report `[seq, d_model]` inputs and outputs on `apply(mdl, X)`.
- Update `docs/are-we-driven-yet.md`: move Named Axes,
  Shape-Checked Arrays, Structured Errors, and `:describe` from
  CONS to PLAN/HAVE.
- Update `docs/plan.md` to mark Saga 11.5 COMPLETE.
- Bump REPL banners to v0.7.5.
- Rebuild and deploy `pages/`.
- Tag `v0.7.5-named-axes`.

## Planned steps

| # | Slug | Phase | What it delivers |
|---|------|-------|------------------|
| 001 | labeled-shape-type | 1 | `LabeledShape` + `labels()` built-in; no behavior change |
| 002 | label-introduction | 2 | `label(x, [...])`, annotation syntax on assignment |
| 003 | label-reshape-rules | 2 | transpose preserves, reshape clears, `reshape_labeled` added |
| 004 | label-propagation-elementwise | 3 | +, -, *, / propagate and validate labels |
| 005 | label-propagation-matmul-reduce | 3 | matmul, reduce_add, softmax by axis name |
| 006 | shape-mismatch-error | 4 | Structured `ShapeMismatch` error variant + rendering |
| 007 | describe-labels-trace | 5 | `:describe` / `:vars` learn labels; trace JSON includes axis labels |
| 008 | named-axes-tutorial-lesson | 6 | New lesson + Model DSL lesson refresh |
| 009 | named-axes-release-v075 | 6 | docs, banners, release tag |

Nine steps. The original plan had ten; step 007's REPL
introspection commands (`:describe`, `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`)
already shipped in Saga 11, so phase 5 is now scoped to teach
those commands about labels and add labels to the trace export.
Expect some merging once implementation starts -- steps 004 and

## Success criteria

Zero behavior change: every existing demo and test must pass unchanged. TDD: add a labels() built-in test, then implement."

content.content: Advanced to step 001-labeled-shape-type.

toolUseResult.stdout: Advanced to step 001-labeled-shape-type.

input.command:
agentrail add --slug label-introduction --prompt "Phase 2: Give users a way to attach labels. Add label(x, [\"batch\", \"time\", \"dim\"]) built-in that tags an existing array. Add annotation syntax on assignment: x : [batch, time, dim] = randn(7, [8, 16, 32]). The annotation is optional; omitted means positional. Parser impact is contained: the colon in assignment is new. TDD: test labels round-trip through label() and annotation syntax; verify unlabeled arrays are unchanged." && \
agentrail add --slug label-reshape-rules --prompt "Phase 2 cont.: Label propagation through shape-changing ops. transpose(x) / x.T preserves labels but swaps their order. reshape(x, ...) clears labels by default (reshape loses semantic axis identity). Add reshape_labeled(x, new_shape, new_labels) opt-in for explicit re-labeling. TDD: transpose keeps label set with swapped order; reshape drops labels; reshape_labeled requires matching rank." && \
agentrail add --slug label-propagation-elementwise --prompt "Phase 3: Elementwise ops propagate and validate labels. +, -, *, / require matching labels when both operands are labeled; union in the usual broadcasting order. Scalar broadcast works unchanged. Row-vector-vs-matrix broadcast works when only one side is labeled; two labeled sides with compatible dims but different labels raise a structured error. Add an explicit relabel(x, [...]) escape hatch. TDD: label match succeeds, mismatch errors, unlabeled-unlabeled unchanged, mixed labeled/unlabeled adopts labels from the labeled side." && \
agentrail add --slug label-propagation-matmul-reduce --prompt "Phase 3 cont.: matmul, reduce_add, softmax propagate and validate labels. matmul(a, b) requires the contraction axis label to match (e.g. [seq, d] @ [d, heads] -> [seq, heads]). reduce_add(x, axis) and friends accept either an integer axis or an axis name: reduce_add(x, \"time\"). softmax(x, \"d_k\") and softmax(x, 1) both work. TDD: matmul with matching contraction axis; matmul mismatch errors; reduce_add/softmax by axis name produce the same result as by integer index." && \
agentrail add --slug shape-mismatch-error --prompt "Phase 4: Replace string EvalError::Unsupported shape errors with a dedicated variant: EvalError::ShapeMismatch { op, expected: LabeledShape, actual: LabeledShape }. Render as a rich REPL message and as a JSON object when an environment flag is set. The web REPL's error formatter learns the new variant and shows a one-line \"expected ... got ... at axis ...\" summary with the offending op name. Migrate the existing broadcasting-failure site in mlpl-eval::eval_ops to the new variant. TDD: broadcasting mismatch emits ShapeMismatch; matmul mismatch emits ShapeMismatch; REPL renders the message."

input.command:
agentrail add --slug describe-labels-trace --prompt "Phase 5: Teach the existing Saga 11 REPL introspection commands about labels, and thread labels into the trace export. :describe <name> learns to print a variable's labeled shape alongside its raw dims. For models it prints the ModelSpec tree with per-layer labeled shapes on the input/output, pinned from the first apply call. :vars output includes labeled shape for every labeled array. trace JSON export includes axis labels so downstream tooling can reason about shape flow. TDD: :describe on a labeled array prints labels; trace JSON round-trips labels." && \
agentrail add --slug named-axes-tutorial-lesson --prompt "Phase 6: Add a \"Named Axes\" tutorial lesson to apps/mlpl-web/src/tutorial.rs immediately after \"Matrices\" but before \"Linear Algebra\", so every subsequent lesson can use labels. Update the Model Composition lesson and the moons/transformer demos to label their inputs and outputs. The Model DSL itself should report [seq, d_model] inputs and outputs on apply(mdl, X). Rebuild pages/ via scripts/build-pages.sh. Commit via /mw-cp." && \
agentrail add --slug named-axes-release-v075 --prompt "Phase 6 wrap-up: Release v0.7.5-named-axes. Mark Saga 11.5 COMPLETE in docs/saga.md, move it into the completed table in docs/status.md and into the completed-sagas list in docs/plan.md, and point next at Saga 12 (Tokenizers/datasets/experiment tracking). Update docs/are-we-driven-yet.md: move Named Axes, Shape-Checked Arrays, and Structured Errors from CONS/PART/PLAN to HAVE. Update docs/milestone-named-axes.md to reflect final state. Bump workspace version to 0.7.5 and REPL banners to v0.7.5 across mlpl-repl + mlpl-web. Rebuild pages/ via scripts/build-pages.sh. Create and push annotated tag v0.7.5-named-axes. Commit via /mw-cp and mark saga done."

chore(agentrail): archive mlpl-modeldsl, init mlpl-named-axes

Archives the completed Saga 11 Model DSL agentrail tree to
.agentrail-archive/mlpl-modeldsl-20260411T124743/ (saga status:
Completed, all 9 steps shipped, tag v0.7.0-modeldsl pushed)
following the same pattern as mlpl-optim-20260408T155006 and
the other nine archived sagas.

Initializes a fresh .agentrail/ for Saga 11.5 ("mlpl-named-axes")
using docs/milestone-named-axes.md as the plan. Pre-populates all
9 steps from the updated plan:

  001 labeled-shape-type              (Phase 1)
  002 label-introduction              (Phase 2)
  003 label-reshape-rules             (Phase 2)
  004 label-propagation-elementwise   (Phase 3)
  005 label-propagation-matmul-reduce (Phase 3)
  006 shape-mismatch-error            (Phase 4)
  007 describe-labels-trace           (Phase 5, scoped-down from
                                       the original 10-step plan
                                       since :describe/:vars/:fns
                                       already shipped in Saga 11)
  008 named-axes-tutorial-lesson      (Phase 6)
  009 named-axes-release-v075         (Phase 6)

Each step's prompt is lifted verbatim from the corresponding
milestone-named-axes.md phase so a fresh agent session can pick
up context without re-reading this conversation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push

content.text:
Both done. Three commits pushed:

1. **`3fe47d8` docs sync** — fixed 3 stale rows in `are-we-driven-yet.md` (differentiable `apply` → HAVE, tutorial lesson count, `:describe`/`:vars`/`:fns` → HAVE) and rescoped `milestone-named-axes.md` from 10 steps to 9 (phase 5 now teaches existing REPL commands about labels instead of introducing them).

2. **`54d2bb9`** — earlier Saga 11 release docs (from prior turn).

3. **`e0eba47` agentrail rotation** — archived `mlpl-modeldsl` → `.agentrail-archive/mlpl-modeldsl-20260411T124743/` and initialized `mlpl-named-axes` with all 9 steps pre-populated. `agentrail status` shows current_step=1, first step is `001-labeled-shape-type`.

Ready to pick up Saga 11.5 in a fresh session with `agentrail next`.

message.content: is are-we-driven-yet.md updated? does it have a list of completed and future features?

content.content:
1	# Are We Driven Yet?
2	
3	**Purpose.** Track MLPL's progress against the two forcing-function
4	documents that drive its long-range design:
5	
6	- `docs/paper-driven-development.txt` -- a curated backlog of ~20
7	  recent ML papers (Dec 2025 - Apr 2026) and the MLPL primitives
8	  each one would exercise.
9	- `docs/ai-agent-driven-development.txt` -- a wishlist from the
10	  perspective of an LLM/agent that wants to use MLPL as its
11	  reasoning and execution substrate.
12	
13	Both documents are speculative discussions, not plans of record.
14	This file is the accounting sheet that turns them into actionable
15	status: for every capability they call out, does MLPL already have
16	it, is it planned in `docs/plan.md`, or does it still need to be
17	shaped into a saga?
18	
19	## Legend
20	
21	| Symbol | Meaning |
22	|--------|---------|
23	| `HAVE` | Shipped, exercised by demos/tests today |
24	| `PART` | Partially implemented, needs deepening to satisfy the source doc |
25	| `PLAN` | Already on the Saga 12-19 roadmap in `docs/plan.md` |
26	| `CONS` | Not planned; needs design + a saga shape before it can move |
27	
28	**Source** column: `P` = from `paper-driven-development.txt`, `A` =
29	from `ai-agent-driven-development.txt`, `P+A` = both.
30	
31	---
32	
33	## 1. Core language and array semantics
34	
35	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
36	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
37	| Dense tensors with row-major storage | A | HAVE | `mlpl-array` |
38	| Scalar broadcasting | A | HAVE | Scalar op array across all primitives |
39	| Element-wise arithmetic and reductions | A | HAVE | `reduce_add`/`reduce_mul` with axis arg |
40	| Matrix multiplication, transpose | A | HAVE | `matmul`, `transpose`, `dot` |
41	| Shape introspection (`shape`, `rank`) | A | HAVE | Built-ins |
42	| Repeat loop and `train N { }` sugar | P+A | HAVE | Saga 10 |
43	| Small core language (few, composable primitives) | A | HAVE | Intentional design constraint |
44	| Canonical formatting of MLPL source | A | CONS | No MLPL-level formatter exists; Rust side uses rustfmt. Needed for LLM roundtripping |
45	| REPL with incremental execution | A | HAVE | `mlpl-repl` + web REPL |
46	| Named axes on tensors (`x[batch, time, dim]`) | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 (`docs/milestone-named-axes.md`) |
47	| Shape-checked / dependent-shape types (`Tensor[B, T, D]`) | A | PART | Saga 11.5 tracks labels as metadata with runtime validation, stopping short of full dependent types |
48	| Dual syntax: terse APL mode vs explicit agent-friendly mode | A | CONS | Open design question |
49	| Code-as-data: `parse`, `transform`, `eval` primitives | A | CONS | Needed for meta-programming, self-modification |
50	| Deterministic execution mode (`with deterministic { }`) | A | CONS | Seed control needs a scoped construct |
51	| Deterministic serialization for programs + models | A | CONS | Needed for repeatable agent loops |
52	| Notebook mode with persisted cell state | A | CONS | Web REPL has session state but no cell model |
53	
54	## 2. Autograd, optimizers, training
55	
56	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
57	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
58	| Reverse-mode autograd (`grad(expr, wrt)`) | A | HAVE | Saga 9 |
59	| `param[shape]` trainable-leaf constructor | A | HAVE | Saga 9 |
60	| `momentum_sgd`, `adam` with persistent state | P+A | HAVE | Saga 10 |
61	| `cosine_schedule`, `linear_warmup` helpers | P | HAVE | Saga 10 |
62	| Model DSL (`linear`/`chain`/activations) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 11, training through `apply()` wired 2026-04-09 |
63	| Model DSL: `residual` / `rms_norm` / `attention` forward | P | HAVE | Saga 11 steps 004-005 |
64	| Model DSL: differentiable `apply()` through residual/norm/attention | P | HAVE | Tape-lowered for linear/chain/activations/residual/rms_norm/single-head attention in Saga 11 step 007 (fb90fb4). Multi-head (`heads>1`) still forward-only pending per-head slicing |
65	| `jacobian f`, `vmap f` | A | CONS | Not on the Saga 11 roadmap; Jax-style transforms would be a Saga 11.5 item |
66	| Named-parameter trees and parameter addressing | P | PART | `ModelSpec::params()` returns a flat list; no path-addressed subtree ops |
67	| Hyperparameter sweep DSL (adapter/rank/lr grid) | P | PLAN | Saga 12 ("experiment tracking") |
68	| Experiment registry + comparison plots | P | PLAN | Saga 12 |
69	| Hessian / eigenvalue / curvature probes | P | CONS | Paper #5 (Learning Rate Matters) asks for this; no plan |
70	| LoRA / QLoRA adapter declarations | P | PLAN | Saga 15 |
71	| 4-bit / low-precision quantization | P | PLAN | Saga 15 |
72	| Self-distillation pipeline primitives (sample -> filter -> finetune) | P | CONS | Papers #17-18; not on roadmap |
73	| RLVR / token-level credit assignment | P | CONS | Paper #17; not on roadmap |
74	
75	## 3. Datasets and data pipelines
76	
77	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
78	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
79	| Synthetic datasets (`moons`, `blobs`, `circles`, `randn`) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 8/10 |
80	| `load.csv`, generic file loaders | A | PLAN | Saga 12 |
81	| Streaming / lazy dataset ops (`shuffle`, `batch`, `map`) | A | PLAN | Saga 12 |
82	| Byte-level BPE tokenizer | P | PLAN | Saga 12 |
83	| Long-context / 100M-token loader | P | CONS | Paper #13 (MSA); needs backend work |
84	| External memory store (key-value table, writeback, sparse addressing) | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("engram memory") covers the idea; no concrete API |
85	
86	## 4. Visualization, tracing, demos
87	
88	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
89	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
90	| Inline SVG visualization (scatter/line/bar/heatmap) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 7 |
91	| Analysis helpers (`hist`, `scatter_labeled`, `loss_curve`, `confusion_matrix`, `boundary_2d`) | P+A | HAVE | Saga 8 |
92	| Execution trace with JSON export | A | HAVE | `mlpl-trace` |
93	| Yew/WASM web REPL | P+A | HAVE | Saga 5 |
94	| Tutorial mode in web REPL | A | HAVE | 20 lessons including "Model Composition (the Model DSL)" added in Saga 11 |
95	| Numeric output summarization (collapsible stats) | A | HAVE | Added 2026-04-09 |
96	| Embedding viz: PCA / t-SNE / UMAP projections | P+A | PLAN | Saga 16 |
97	| 3D scatter via SVG projection, nearest-neighbor links | P | PLAN | Saga 16 |
98	| Topology / block-diagram view of model graphs | P | CONS | Paper #2 (mHC) wants it; needs a `ModelSpec` -> SVG pass |
99	| Debate / "society of thought" transcript viewer | P | CONS | Paper #19; not on roadmap |
100	| Skill graph + reuse frequency chart | P | CONS | Paper #10; not on roadmap |
101	| Kernel performance leaderboard + diff view | P | CONS | Paper #12 (CUDA Agent); not on roadmap |
102	| PaperBanana-style slide generation from a run | P | CONS | Artifact generator; not on roadmap |
103	
104	## 5. Backends and runtime integrations
105	
106	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
107	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
108	| CPU dense kernels (current default) | A | HAVE | `mlpl-array` |
109	| Explicit device placement syntax (`x \|> cuda`, `with mlx { }`) | A | CONS | Required by both source docs; no construct chosen yet |
110	| MLX backend | P+A | PLAN | Saga 14 |
111	| Lazy execution graph + kernel fusion | P | PLAN | Saga 14 |
112	| CUDA backend | P+A | PLAN | Saga 17 |
113	| Distributed execution (`run model on nodes[...]`) | P | PLAN | Saga 17 |
114	| Ollama / llama.cpp unified model interface | P+A | PLAN | Saga 19 (REST client built-ins) |
115	| Streaming `generate` with incremental tokens | A | CONS | Saga 19 covers REST; streaming as a language construct is not pinned down |
116	| Profiler ingestion / FLOPs estimation | P+A | CONS | Paper #12 wants it as a reward signal; no plan |
117	
118	## 6. Agent-facing ergonomics
119	
120	| Capability | Source | Status | Notes |
121	|------------|--------|--------|-------|
122	| Introspectable execution graph (`trace f`) | A | HAVE | `mlpl-trace` JSON export |
123	| Structured errors (kind + expected/actual fields) | A | PLAN | Saga 11.5 phase 4 introduces a structured `ShapeMismatch` error variant and a JSON error channel |
124	| Built-in testing primitives (`assert`, `approx_equal`, `fuzz`) | A | CONS | Rust tests exist, MLPL-level asserts do not |
125	| Sandbox execution with time/memory/GPU limits (`run_safe`) | A | CONS | Needed for agent self-correction loops; no plan |
126	| Cost / performance introspection (`profile f x`) | A | CONS | Paper #12 also benefits; no plan |
127	| `:describe <name>` / `:vars` / `:fns` REPL introspection | A | HAVE | Shipped in Saga 11 (99f7c8a, 623652d): `:vars`, `:models`, `:fns` user / `:builtins`, `:wsid`, `:describe`, `:help <topic>` in both `mlpl-repl` and `mlpl-web` |
128	| `explain f` structured-explanation primitive | A | CONS | Speculative but high-leverage for agent tooling |
129	| `repair f given error` primitive | A | CONS | Speculative; depends on structured errors + code-as-data |
130	| `optimize f for gpu` primitive | A | CONS | Speculative; depends on device placement + profile |
131	| Tool schemas and tool-call execution | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("ICL/ICRL") + Saga 19 |
132	| Trajectory / episode / rollout / reward records | P | PLAN | Saga 18 |
133	| Controller/worker multi-model orchestration | P | PLAN | Saga 18 ("multi-model orchestration") |
134	| Replay buffers and curriculum control | P | PLAN | Saga 18 |
135	
136	## 7. Paper-specific primitives (the 20-paper backlog)
137	
138	Papers from `docs/paper-driven-development.txt` ranked backlog. Each
139	row is "what MLPL would need to make this paper a one-screen demo."
140	
141	| # | Paper | MLPL primitive asked for | Status |
142	|---|-------|--------------------------|--------|
143	| 1 | Recursive Language Models | Recursive task/decompose/reduce constructs; scheduler for nested subcalls with budget + memoization | CONS |
144	| 2 | mHC: Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections | First-class model-graph syntax for residual-stream variants and constrained projection ops | PART (ModelSpec exists, constrained projections do not) |
145	| 3 | Conditional Memory via Scalable Lookup | Memory tables, lookup, writeback, sparse addressing as first-class ops | PLAN (Saga 18) |
146	| 4 | In-Context RL for Tool Use | `episode`, `rollout`, `reward`, `tool`, fewshot curriculum syntax | PLAN (Saga 18) |
147	| 5 | Learning Rate Matters (LoRA tuning) | Sweep DSL (`adapter`, `rank`, `lr`, `scheduler`, `seed`, `judge`) | PLAN (Saga 12 + 15) |
148	| 6 | RelayLLM | Controller/worker decoding, token-level help requests, cooperative generation | PLAN (Saga 18 + 19) |
149	| 7 | CM2 Checklist Rewards | Declarative checklist reward specs with evidence anchors | CONS |
150	| 8 | PEARL planner/executor | Separate `plan { }` and `execute { }` stages with distinct rewards | CONS |
151	| 9 | Tool-R0 self-evolving agents | Self-play / dual-agent curriculum loops | CONS |
152	| 10 | SkillRL | `skill` as a reusable packaged abstraction (code + prompt + tests + permissions) | CONS |
153	| 11 | Agent World Model | Environment DSL for executable synthetic worlds and tools | CONS |
154	| 12 | CUDA Agent | Kernel blocks, verification hooks, profiler-aware reward terms | PART-PLAN (Saga 17 provides CUDA; codegen loop is unplanned) |
155	| 13 | MSA (100M-token attention) | Long-context memory/attention operators; memory-interleaving | CONS |
156	| 14 | Self-Indexing KVCache | Cache-policy syntax, programmable inference-time memory layout | CONS |
157	| 15 | S3-Attention | `retrieve` from attention primitives blurring retrieval + inference | CONS |
158	| 16 | iGRPO self-feedback | `draft`, `select_best`, `refine` syntax for two-stage loops | CONS |
159	| 17 | Self-Distilled RLVR | Token-level credit assignment and distilled-target generation | CONS |
160	| 18 | Self-distillation for code | `sample -> filter -> finetune` pipeline primitives | CONS |
161	| 19 | Societies of Thought | `voices`, `debate`, `reconcile`, `critic`, `judge` multi-perspective syntax | CONS |
162	| 20 | Attention Residuals | Residual-policy syntax; skip/accumulate as first-class choice | PART (ModelSpec has `residual`; no policy selection) |
163	
164	---
165	
166	## Aggregate scoreboard
167	
168	| Bucket | Count | What it means |
169	|--------|-------|---------------|
170	| HAVE   | ~28   | Shipped, exercised by demos/tests |
171	| PART   | ~5    | Partially there; needs deepening (notably multi-head attention tape lowering, named-parameter tree addressing, mHC architecture graph) |
172	| PLAN   | ~17   | On the Sagas 11.5 / 12-19 roadmap |
173	| CONS   | ~30   | Not on the roadmap; each is a candidate for a new saga or an inserted step |
174	
175	## What this tells us about priorities
176	
177	Historical note (2026-04-09): the three clusters originally listed
178	here were (1) named axes + shape types, (2) structured errors +
179	trace-driven self-correction, and (3) differentiable `apply`
180	through residual/rms_norm/attention. Cluster 3 shipped in Saga 11
181	steps 006 and 007. The REPL-introspection subset of cluster 2
182	(`:vars`, `:models`, `:fns`, `:wsid`, `:describe`) also shipped in
183	Saga 11 ahead of schedule. Clusters 1 and the remaining structured
184	errors half of cluster 2 landed as **Saga 11.5 -- Named axes and
